\texttt{setSubFilter(COSName)} & \texttt{/SubFilter} & Yes & The
encoding format of the signature value. For PAdES, use
\texttt{ETSI.CAdES.detached}. For legacy compatibility,
\texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached}. The deprecated \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.sha1}
embeds the document hash as CMS content --- do not use it in new
code. \\
\texttt{setSignDate(Calendar)} & \texttt{/M} & No & Claimed signing
time. This is \emph{not} the authoritative timestamp --- it is the
signer's local clock. A TSA timestamp (PAdES-B-T) provides cryptographic
proof of time. Set it anyway, because viewers display it when no TSA
timestamp exists. \\
\texttt{setName(String)} & \texttt{/Name} & No & Signer's name as a
display string. PAdES validators typically extract the name from the
signing certificate's CN instead. Still useful for the human reader. \\
\texttt{setReason(String)} & \texttt{/Reason} & No & Why the document
was signed. Free text. Some workflows require specific values
("Approved", "Reviewed"). \\
\texttt{setLocation(String)} & \texttt{/Location} & No & Where the
document was signed. Free text. "Berlin, Germany" or "Server Room
3" --- whatever is meaningful. \\
\texttt{setContactInfo(String)} & \texttt{/ContactInfo} & No & Contact
information for the signer. Rarely used in practice, but mandated by
some government workflows. \\
\texttt{setByteRange(int{[}{]})} & \texttt{/ByteRange} & Auto & The byte
ranges of the signed content. \emph{You do not set this yourself.}
PDFBox computes it during \texttt{saveIncremental()}. If you manually
set it, you will break the signature. \\
\end{longtable}

Do not call \texttt{setByteRange()} manually. PDFBox calculates the byte
range during the incremental save process. If you set it yourself, the
byte range will not match the actual file offsets after writing, and the
signature will be invalid. We have seen production code that does
this --- it always fails.

\subsection{SignatureInterface: The Cryptographic
Callback}\label{_signatureinterface_the_cryptographic_callback}

\texttt{SignatureInterface} is a single-method interface:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{interface}\NormalTok{ SignatureInterface }\OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{sign}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

PDFBox calls your \texttt{sign()} method during
\texttt{saveIncremental()}. The \texttt{InputStream} parameter contains
the bytes identified by the \texttt{/ByteRange} --- that is, the entire
PDF file \emph{minus} the gap reserved for the \texttt{/Contents} hex
string. Your job is to read those bytes, produce a CMS
\texttt{SignedData} structure (or whatever your \texttt{/SubFilter}
requires), and return the DER-encoded result.

The returned \texttt{byte{[}{]}} is hex-encoded and written into the
\texttt{/Contents} field of the signature dictionary. If the returned
bytes are larger than the space reserved for \texttt{/Contents}, PDFBox
throws an exception. If they are smaller, the remaining space is
zero-padded.

The \texttt{InputStream} you receive in \texttt{sign()} is the
concatenation of the two byte ranges --- the bytes before the
\texttt{/Contents} gap and the bytes after it. You must read \emph{all}
of them with \texttt{content.readAllBytes()} (or equivalent). If you
only read part of the stream, you are signing incomplete data, and the
signature will fail validation.

Here is the simplest possible implementation --- it signs the content
with RSA/SHA-256 and produces a basic CMS \texttt{SignedData}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ SimpleCmsSignatureInterface }\KeywordTok{implements}\NormalTok{ SignatureInterface }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{final} \BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ privateKey}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{final} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{final} \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{;}

    \KeywordTok{public} \FunctionTok{SimpleCmsSignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}
                                        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}
                                        \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{privateKey} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ privateKey}\OperatorTok{;}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{certificate} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{;}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{chain} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \AttributeTok{@Override}
    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{sign}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \CommentTok{// Read all byte{-}range content}
            \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ contentBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{readAllBytes}\OperatorTok{();}

            \CommentTok{// Build the CMS SignedData (Chapter 30 covers this in full detail)}
\NormalTok{            CMSSignedDataGenerator gen }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedDataGenerator}\OperatorTok{();}

\NormalTok{            ContentSigner signer }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaContentSignerBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"SHA256withRSA"}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{privateKey}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{            DigestCalculatorProvider dcProvider }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaDigestCalculatorProviderBuilder}\OperatorTok{()}
                            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{();}

\NormalTok{            gen}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignerInfoGenerator}\OperatorTok{(}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaSignerInfoGeneratorBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{dcProvider}\OperatorTok{)}
                            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signer}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{));}

\NormalTok{            gen}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addCertificates}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaCertStore}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{chain}\OperatorTok{));}

\NormalTok{            CMSProcessableByteArray cmsContent }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSProcessableByteArray}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{contentBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            CMSSignedData signedData }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ gen}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{generate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cmsContent}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{);} 

            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ signedData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getEncoded}\OperatorTok{();}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{catch} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Exception}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{throw} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{IOException}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"CMS signing failed: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMessage}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  The \texttt{false} parameter means "detached signature" --- the
  content is not embedded inside the CMS structure. This is mandatory
  for PDF signatures: the content lives in the PDF file itself, not
  inside the CMS blob.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{SignatureOptions: Container Size and Visual
Appearance}\label{_signatureoptions_container_size_and_visual_appearance}

\texttt{SignatureOptions} controls two things: the size of the
\texttt{/Contents} container (how many bytes are reserved in the PDF for
the CMS signature) and the visual appearance stream.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{SignatureOptions sigOptions }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{sigOptions}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{32768}\OperatorTok{);} \CommentTok{// 32 KB for the CMS container}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{preferredSignatureSize} is the number of \emph{bytes}
reserved for the hex-encoded signature value in \texttt{/Contents}.
Since the hex encoding doubles the size, a 32768-byte container can hold
a CMS blob of up to about 16 KB.

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3000}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2000}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.5000}}@{}}
\caption{Recommended Container Sizes}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Scenario
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Size (bytes)
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Why
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Scenario
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Size (bytes)
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Why
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
Basic signature (no timestamp) & 32768 & A CMS \texttt{SignedData} with
one signer, RSA-2048, and a 3-certificate chain typically occupies 4-8
KB. 32 KB gives ample room. \\
Signature with TSA timestamp (PAdES-B-T) & 65536 & The timestamp token
adds another CMS \texttt{SignedData} structure (the TSA's signature, its
certificate chain, and the \texttt{TSTInfo}). 64 KB handles even large
TSA responses. \\
Signature with embedded OCSP/CRL (PAdES-B-LT) & 131072 or more & CRL
responses can be very large (100 KB+). If you embed revocation data in
the CMS structure rather than the DSS, you need a correspondingly large
container. \\
\end{longtable}

If your CMS blob exceeds the reserved container size,
\texttt{saveIncremental()} throws an \texttt{IOException} with a message
like "can't write signature, not enough space." The fix is to increase
\texttt{preferredSignatureSize}. But do not set it to absurd values
(e.g., 10 MB) because the entire container is written as hex zeros into
the PDF --- a 10 MB container adds 20 MB of hex padding to the file.

\section{Complete Basic Signing
Example}\label{_complete_basic_signing_example}

Let us put the three pieces together into a complete, compilable
example. This is the minimum code needed to sign a PDF with PDFBox
3.0.0.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{FileInputStream}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{IOException}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{InputStream}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{nio}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{file}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Files}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{nio}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{file}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Path}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{KeyStore}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PrivateKey}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Enumeration}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{List}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Loader}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDDocument}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaCertStore}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{CMSProcessableByteArray}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{CMSSignedDataGenerator}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaSignerInfoGeneratorBuilder}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{provider}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{BouncyCastleProvider}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{operator}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{ContentSigner}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{operator}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaContentSignerBuilder}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{operator}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaDigestCalculatorProviderBuilder}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Minimal PDF signing example with PDFBox }\CommentTok{3.}\NormalTok{x and BouncyCastle}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Signs a PDF with an invisible signature using a PKCS}\CommentTok{\#12}\NormalTok{ keystore}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ BasicPdfSigner }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{main}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ args}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// Register BouncyCastle provider {-}{-} must happen before any crypto operation}
\NormalTok{        java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{Security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{BouncyCastleProvider}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{        Path inputPdf }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Path}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{of}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"document.pdf"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        Path keystorePath }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Path}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{of}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"keystore.p12"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{char}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ keystorePassword }\OperatorTok{=} \StringTok{"changeit"}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toCharArray}\OperatorTok{();}

        \CommentTok{// Load key material from PKCS\#12}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\NormalTok{ ks }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"PKCS12"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{FileInputStream}\NormalTok{ fis }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{FileInputStream}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{keystorePath}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toFile}\OperatorTok{()))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{fis}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ keystorePassword}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \BuiltInTok{Enumeration}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{String}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ aliases }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{aliases}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ alias }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ aliases}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{nextElement}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ privateKey }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getKey}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alias}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ keystorePassword}\OperatorTok{);}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ cert }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alias}\OperatorTok{);}

        \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ chain }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
        \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{Certificate}\NormalTok{ c }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificateChain}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alias}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            chain}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{((}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ c}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Load the PDF}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Files}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{readAllBytes}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{inputPdf}\OperatorTok{);}

        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{} 
            \CommentTok{// Create the signature dictionary}
\NormalTok{            PDSignature signature }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setName}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getName}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setReason}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Document approval"}\OperatorTok{);}

            \CommentTok{// Configure container size}
\NormalTok{            SignatureOptions options }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            options}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{32768}\OperatorTok{);}

            \CommentTok{// Wire everything together}
\NormalTok{            SignatureInterface sigInterface }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SimpleCmsSignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signature}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sigInterface}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ options}\OperatorTok{);} 

            \CommentTok{// Save incrementally {-}{-} this triggers the sign() callback}
            \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ baos }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncremental}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{baos}\OperatorTok{);} 

            \CommentTok{// Write the signed PDF}
\NormalTok{            Path outputPdf }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Path}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{of}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"document{-}signed.pdf"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            Files}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{write}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{outputPdf}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ baos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toByteArray}\OperatorTok{());}

            \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{out}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{println}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Signed PDF written to "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ outputPdf}
                    \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" ("} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ baos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" bytes)"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  PDFBox 3.x uses \texttt{Loader.loadPDF(byte{[}{]})}. The old
  \texttt{PDDocument.load()} is gone. See the migration section later in
  this chapter.
\item
  \texttt{addSignature()} registers the signature dictionary, the
  callback, and the options. Nothing is written yet.
\item
  \texttt{saveIncremental()} writes the PDF as an incremental update,
  computes the byte range, calls your \texttt{sign()} method,
  hex-encodes the result, and patches it into the \texttt{/Contents}
  field. This is where all the magic happens.
\end{itemize}

Always use \texttt{saveIncremental()}, never \texttt{save()}. A regular
\texttt{save()} rewrites the entire PDF, which destroys any existing
signatures. \texttt{saveIncremental()} appends a new revision to the
file, preserving all previous signatures. This is not optional --- it is
the only correct way to save a signed PDF.

\section{The InputStream in sign(): What You Are Actually
Signing}\label{_the_inputstream_in_sign_what_you_are_actually_signing}

The \texttt{InputStream} passed to your \texttt{sign()} method is one of
the most misunderstood parts of the PDFBox signing API. Let us be very
precise about what it contains.

When PDFBox writes the PDF during \texttt{saveIncremental()}, it creates
the file with a placeholder for the \texttt{/Contents} value. The byte
range is computed as two ranges that together cover the entire file
\emph{except} the \texttt{/Contents} hex string:

\begin{verbatim}
/ByteRange [0 offset1 offset2 length2]
\end{verbatim}

Where:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Range 1: bytes 0 through \texttt{offset1} (everything before the
  \texttt{/Contents} hex string)
\item
  Range 2: bytes \texttt{offset2} through \texttt{offset2\ +\ length2}
  (everything after the \texttt{/Contents} hex string)
\end{itemize}

The \texttt{InputStream} you receive is the concatenation of these two
ranges. You do not see the gap. You do not need to parse the byte range.
PDFBox handles all of that.

Your \texttt{sign()} method receives a continuous stream of bytes, and
you sign all of them.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\AttributeTok{@Override}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{sign}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ contentBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{readAllBytes}\OperatorTok{();} 
    \CommentTok{// contentBytes now contains Range1 + Range2}
    \CommentTok{// Sign these bytes and return the CMS SignedData}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  You \emph{must} read the entire stream. If you read only part of it
  (e.g., using a fixed-size buffer and stopping early), the hash you
  compute will not match the hash a validator computes over the full
  byte range, and the signature will be invalid.
\end{itemize}

A common bug in PDFBox 2.x code (which people copy into 3.x projects) is
using \texttt{IOUtils.toByteArray()} from Apache Commons IO with a size
limit. The byte range content can be large --- for a 100 MB PDF, the
stream is approximately 100 MB. If your byte array buffer is smaller,
you get a truncated hash and a broken signature that validates in your
tests (small PDFs) but fails in production (large PDFs).

\section{Visible Signature
Appearances}\label{_visible_signature_appearances}

An invisible signature is fine for server-side signing workflows, but
many use cases --- contracts, approvals, notarizations --- require a
visible signature block on a specific page. This section shows how to
create visible appearances with PDFBox.

\subsection{The Form XObject Approach}\label{_the_form_xobject_approach}

A signature appearance is a Form XObject --- a self-contained mini-page
with its own coordinate system, resources, and content stream. You build
it, attach it to the signature field's widget annotation, and PDFBox
includes it in the signed content.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDDocument}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDResources}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{common}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDRectangle}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{font}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{font}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{graphics}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDFormXObject}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{OutputStream}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{nio}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{charset}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{StandardCharsets}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Builds a visible text}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{based signature appearance as a Form XObject}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ SignatureAppearanceBuilder }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{/**}
     \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Creates and attaches a visible text appearance to a signature field}\CommentTok{.}
     \CommentTok{*}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{doc}\CommentTok{      }\NormalTok{the document being signed}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{sigField}\CommentTok{ }\NormalTok{the signature field to attach the appearance to}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{name}\CommentTok{     }\NormalTok{signer name to display}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{reason}\CommentTok{   }\NormalTok{signing reason to display}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{width}\CommentTok{    }\NormalTok{appearance width in points}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{height}\CommentTok{   }\NormalTok{appearance height in points}
     \CommentTok{*/}
    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{buildTextAppearance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                            PDSignatureField sigField}\OperatorTok{,}
                                            \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ name}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ reason}\OperatorTok{,}
                                            \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ width}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ height}\OperatorTok{)}
            \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{        PDResources resources }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDResources}\OperatorTok{();}

        \CommentTok{// Use a Standard 14 font {-}{-} no embedding needed}
\NormalTok{        PDType1Font font }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA}\OperatorTok{);} 
\NormalTok{        org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\NormalTok{ fontName }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ resources}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{font}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Build the content stream}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ fontSize }\OperatorTok{=} \FloatTok{10f}\OperatorTok{;}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ lineSpacing }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ fontSize }\OperatorTok{*} \FloatTok{1.4f}\OperatorTok{;}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ margin }\OperatorTok{=} \FloatTok{4f}\OperatorTok{;}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ y }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ height }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ margin }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ fontSize}\OperatorTok{;}

        \BuiltInTok{StringBuilder}\NormalTok{ ops }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{StringBuilder}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BT}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"/"}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{fontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getName}\OperatorTok{())}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{fontSize}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Tf}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"0 0 0 rg}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);} \CommentTok{// black text}

        \CommentTok{// Line 1: "Digitally signed by:"}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{margin}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{y}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Td}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"(Digitally signed by:) Tj}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Line 2: signer name}
\NormalTok{        y }\OperatorTok{{-}=}\NormalTok{ lineSpacing}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{margin}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{y}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Td}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"("}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\FunctionTok{escapePs}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{name}\OperatorTok{)).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{") Tj}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Line 3: reason}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{reason }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{\&\&} \OperatorTok{!}\NormalTok{reason}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEmpty}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            y }\OperatorTok{{-}=}\NormalTok{ lineSpacing}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{            ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{margin}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{y}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Td}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"(Reason: "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\FunctionTok{escapePs}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{reason}\OperatorTok{)).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{") Tj}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{        ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ET}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}

        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ streamBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toString}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getBytes}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{StandardCharsets}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{US\_ASCII}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Create the Form XObject}
\NormalTok{        PDFormXObject form }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDFormXObject}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setBBox}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDRectangle}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{width}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ height}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{        form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setResources}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{resources}\OperatorTok{);}

        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{OutputStream}\NormalTok{ os }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getStream}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{createOutputStream}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{} 
\NormalTok{            os}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{write}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{streamBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Attach to the widget annotation}
\NormalTok{        org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\NormalTok{ apDict }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new}\NormalTok{ org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        apDict}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{N}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ form}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        sigField}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getWidgets}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{getCOSObject}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{AP}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ apDict}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{/**}\NormalTok{ Escape parentheses and backslashes for a PDF string literal}\CommentTok{. */}
    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{escapePs}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ text}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ text}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{replace}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{)}
                   \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{replace}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"("}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{("}\OperatorTok{)}
                   \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{replace}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{")"}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}\textbackslash{}}\StringTok{)"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  In PDFBox 3.x, \texttt{PDType1Font.HELVETICA} does not exist. You must
  use \texttt{new\ PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA)}.
  This is one of the most common migration errors. See the migration
  section at the end of this chapter.
\item
  \texttt{form.getStream().createOutputStream()} returns an output
  stream that writes directly into the Form XObject's content stream.
  This is the correct way to write appearance content in PDFBox 3.x. You
  cannot use \texttt{PDPageContentStream} with a Form XObject.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Image-Based Appearances}\label{_image_based_appearances}

For handwritten signature images (e.g., from a drawing canvas or a
scanned signature):

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{graphics}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{image}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDImageXObject}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Base64}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Creates a signature appearance from a Base64}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{encoded PNG image}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{buildImageAppearance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                         PDSignatureField sigField}\OperatorTok{,}
                                         \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ base64Png}\OperatorTok{,}
                                         \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ width}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ height}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// Decode the image}
    \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ data }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ base64Png}\OperatorTok{;}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{data}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{contains}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{","}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        data }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ data}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{substring}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{data}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{indexOf}\OperatorTok{(}\CharTok{\textquotesingle{},\textquotesingle{}}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ imageBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Base64}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDecoder}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{decode}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{data}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{    PDImageXObject pdImage }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDImageXObject}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{createFromByteArray}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ imageBytes}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"signature{-}image"}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{    PDResources resources }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDResources}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\NormalTok{ imgName }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ resources}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdImage}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// Scale the image to fit the appearance rectangle while maintaining}
    \CommentTok{// aspect ratio}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ imgW }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ pdImage}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getWidth}\OperatorTok{();}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ imgH }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ pdImage}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getHeight}\OperatorTok{();}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ scale }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Math}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{min}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{width }\OperatorTok{/}\NormalTok{ imgW}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ height }\OperatorTok{/}\NormalTok{ imgH}\OperatorTok{);}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ drawW }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ imgW }\OperatorTok{*}\NormalTok{ scale}\OperatorTok{;}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ drawH }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ imgH }\OperatorTok{*}\NormalTok{ scale}\OperatorTok{;}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ xOffset }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{width }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drawW}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{/} \DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{;}
    \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ yOffset }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{height }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ drawH}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{/} \DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{;}

    \CommentTok{// PDF content stream: push graphics state, apply transformation}
    \CommentTok{// matrix, draw image, restore graphics state}
    \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ ops }\OperatorTok{=} \StringTok{"q}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}
            \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ drawW }\OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" 0 0 "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ drawH }\OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" "}
            \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ xOffset }\OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ yOffset }\OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" cm}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}
            \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{"/"} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ imgName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getName}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" Do}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}
            \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{"Q}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{;}

\NormalTok{    PDFormXObject form }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDFormXObject}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setBBox}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDRectangle}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{width}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ height}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setResources}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{resources}\OperatorTok{);}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{OutputStream}\NormalTok{ os }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getStream}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{createOutputStream}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        os}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{write}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ops}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getBytes}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{nio}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{charset}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{StandardCharsets}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{US\_ASCII}\OperatorTok{));}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\NormalTok{ apDict }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new}\NormalTok{ org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    apDict}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{N}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ form}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    sigField}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getWidgets}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{getCOSObject}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{AP}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ apDict}\OperatorTok{);}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The content stream uses the standard PDF imaging operators: \texttt{q}
(save graphics state), \texttt{cm} (concatenate transformation matrix),
\texttt{Do} (paint XObject), \texttt{Q} (restore graphics state). The
transformation matrix
\texttt{{[}drawW\ 0\ 0\ drawH\ xOffset\ yOffset{]}} scales and positions
the image within the appearance rectangle.

\section{Multi-Signature Support}\label{_multi_signature_support}

A PDF can contain multiple signatures. Each signature is an independent
signature field with its own \texttt{/Sig} dictionary and its own byte
range. The key insight: each subsequent signature covers the
\emph{entire} file as it existed after the previous signature, including
all previous signatures.

\subsection{How Multiple Signatures
Work}\label{_how_multiple_signatures_work}

Consider a document signed by Alice, then by Bob:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Alice signs the original document. Her byte range covers the entire
  file (minus her \texttt{/Contents} gap). PDFBox writes this as an
  incremental update --- the original file plus a new cross-reference
  section and trailer.
\item
  Bob signs the document \emph{after} Alice. His byte range covers the
  entire file as it now exists --- including Alice's incremental update
  and her signature. Bob's signature is written as a \emph{second}
  incremental update appended after Alice's.
\end{enumerate}

Each signature's byte range covers everything that existed when that
signature was created. Alice's signature does not cover Bob's changes
(because Bob had not signed yet), but Bob's signature covers Alice's.

\subsection{Code: Adding a Second
Signature}\label{_code_adding_a_second_signature}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Adds a second signature to an already}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{signed PDF}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ The key is that we load the already}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{signed bytes and use saveIncremental}\CommentTok{()}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ again }\CommentTok{{-}{-}}\NormalTok{ PDFBox appends another revision}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{addSecondSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ alreadySignedPdf}\OperatorTok{,}
                                  \BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}
                                  \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}
                                  \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{,}
                                  \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ fieldName}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alreadySignedPdf}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// Find or create the second signature field}
\NormalTok{        PDAcroForm acroForm }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAcroForm}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        PDSignatureField sigField }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}

        \CommentTok{// Look for an existing empty signature field}
        \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDField field }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ acroForm}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getFieldTree}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{field }\KeywordTok{instanceof}\NormalTok{ PDSignatureField sf}
                    \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ fieldName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{equals}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sf}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getFullyQualifiedName}\OperatorTok{())}
                    \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ sf}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignature}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                sigField }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ sf}\OperatorTok{;}
                \ControlFlowTok{break}\OperatorTok{;}
            \OperatorTok{\}}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sigField }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{throw} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{IllegalStateException}\OperatorTok{(}
                    \StringTok{"No empty signature field found: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ fieldName}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Build the second signature}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature signature }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Bob Jones"}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        sigField}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setValue}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signature}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        SignatureOptions options }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        options}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{32768}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        SignatureInterface sigInterface }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SimpleCmsSignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signature}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sigInterface}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ options}\OperatorTok{);}

        \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ baos }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncremental}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{baos}\OperatorTok{);} 
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ baos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toByteArray}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  Each call to \texttt{saveIncremental()} appends a new revision. The
  file grows with each signature. A document with three signatures has
  four revisions (original + three incremental updates).
\end{itemize}

The order in which signatures are applied matters for validation. Adobe
Acrobat validates each signature against its revision --- it checks that
the bytes covered by Alice's byte range have not been modified. If
someone modified Alice's revision after she signed (other than by
appending a new incremental update), Acrobat flags Alice's signature as
invalid, even if Bob's is fine.

\section{External Signing: HSM and Remote Key
Flows}\label{_external_signing_hsm_and_remote_key_flows}

In many production environments, the private key never leaves a Hardware
Security Module (HSM), a cloud KMS, or a remote signing service. The key
cannot be exported, so you cannot pass it to a
\texttt{JcaContentSignerBuilder}. Instead, you need to split the signing
process: compute the hash locally, send the hash to the remote system,
receive the raw signature value, and assemble the CMS structure
yourself.

\subsection{The Hash-Signing Pattern}\label{_the_hash_signing_pattern}

The flow has three steps:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Prepare}: Create the PDF with a placeholder signature, compute
  the byte range, and extract the bytes to be signed.
\item
  \textbf{Hash}: Compute the SHA-256 digest of the byte range content.
\item
  \textbf{Sign remotely}: Send the hash (not the document) to the
  HSM/cloud service, receive the raw signature bytes, and embed them
  into the CMS structure.
\end{enumerate}

PDFBox supports this through a two-phase API using
\texttt{ExternalSigningSupport}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{ExternalSigningSupport}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Signs a PDF using an external key source }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{HSM}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ cloud KMS}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ smart card}\CommentTok{).}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ The private key never leaves the external system }\CommentTok{{-}{-}}\NormalTok{ only a hash is sent}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{signWithExternalKey}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                   ExternalKeyService externalKeyService}\OperatorTok{,}
                                   \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}
                                   \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ result }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature signature }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{        signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{        SignatureOptions options }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        options}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{32768}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Register signature WITHOUT a SignatureInterface}
\NormalTok{        doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signature}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ options}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// saveIncrementalForExternalSigning writes the PDF with a}
        \CommentTok{// placeholder and returns an ExternalSigningSupport object}
\NormalTok{        ExternalSigningSupport externalSigning }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncrementalForExternalSigning}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{result}\OperatorTok{);} 

        \CommentTok{// Get the byte range content}
        \BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ content }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ externalSigning}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getContent}\OperatorTok{();} 
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ contentBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{readAllBytes}\OperatorTok{();}

        \CommentTok{// Compute the hash locally}
        \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\NormalTok{ digest }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"SHA{-}256"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ hash }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ digest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{digest}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{contentBytes}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Send hash to external system, receive raw signature value}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ rawSignature }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                externalKeyService}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{signHash}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{hash}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"SHA256withRSA"}\OperatorTok{);} 

        \CommentTok{// Build CMS structure around the raw signature}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ cmsBytes }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{buildCmsWithExternalSignature}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                rawSignature}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ contentBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Write the CMS bytes into the placeholder}
\NormalTok{        externalSigning}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cmsBytes}\OperatorTok{);} 
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ result}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toByteArray}\OperatorTok{();}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \texttt{saveIncrementalForExternalSigning()} writes the PDF to the
  output stream with a placeholder \texttt{/Contents} and returns an
  \texttt{ExternalSigningSupport} handle.
\item
  \texttt{getContent()} returns the byte range content as an
  \texttt{InputStream} --- the same data that would be passed to
  \texttt{sign()} in the callback-based approach.
\item
  This is the only operation that touches the external key. You send a
  32-byte SHA-256 hash and receive a raw signature value (typically 256
  bytes for RSA-2048 or 64 bytes for ECDSA P-256).
\item
  \texttt{setSignature()} hex-encodes the CMS bytes and patches them
  into the \texttt{/Contents} placeholder.
\end{itemize}

Building the CMS structure with a pre-computed raw signature is more
involved than using \texttt{CMSSignedDataGenerator}. You need to
construct the \texttt{SignerInfo} manually with the external signature
value. Chapter 30 shows the complete code for this.

\section{Certification Signatures with
DocMDP}\label{_certification_signatures_with_docmdp}

A certification signature (also called a "certifying signature" or "MDP
signature") is a special first signature that declares permission levels
for future modifications to the document. It uses the \texttt{/DocMDP}
transform method (ISO 32000-2:2020 S12.8.2.2).

\subsection{The Three DocMDP Levels}\label{_the_three_docmdp_levels}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.1111}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3333}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.5556}}@{}}
\caption{DocMDP Permission Levels --- ISO 32000-2:2020 S12.8.2.2, Table
257}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Level
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Name
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
What Is Allowed After Certification
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Level
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Name
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
What Is Allowed After Certification
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
1 & No changes & No modifications whatsoever. Even filling in form
fields invalidates the signature. \\
2 & Form fill + sign & Form fields may be filled, signature fields may
be signed, and pages may be instantiated from templates. No other
changes. \\
3 & Form fill + sign + annotate & Everything in level 2, plus
annotations may be added, modified, or deleted. \\
\end{longtable}

Level 2 is by far the most common in practice --- it allows a document
to be certified by the author and then signed by additional parties.

\subsection{Code: Applying a Certification
Signature}\label{_code_applying_a_certification_signature}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSArray}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSInteger}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Applies a certification }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{DocMDP}\CommentTok{)}\NormalTok{ signature to a PDF}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ This must be the FIRST signature in the document}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{applyCertificationSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                         PDSignature signature}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                                         PDSignatureField sigField}\OperatorTok{,}
                                         \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ docMdpLevel}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// Clamp level to valid range}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ level }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{docMdpLevel }\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}=} \DecValTok{1} \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ docMdpLevel }\OperatorTok{\textless{}=} \DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{)}
            \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ docMdpLevel }\OperatorTok{:} \DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{;}

    \CommentTok{// Build the /Reference array with a DocMDP transform}
\NormalTok{    COSDictionary sigRef }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    sigRef}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{TYPE}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"SigRef"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    sigRef}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"TransformMethod"}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{            COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"DocMDP"}\OperatorTok{));}

\NormalTok{    COSDictionary transformParams }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    transformParams}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{TYPE}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"TransformParams"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    transformParams}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"P"}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{            COSInteger}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{level}\OperatorTok{));} 
\NormalTok{    transformParams}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"V"}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{            COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"1.2"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    sigRef}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"TransformParams"}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{            transformParams}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{    COSArray refArray }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{COSArray}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    refArray}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sigRef}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCOSObject}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Reference"}\OperatorTok{),}\NormalTok{ refArray}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// Set /Perms /DocMDP in the document catalog {-}{-} this is what}
    \CommentTok{// tells viewers "this document has a certification signature"}
\NormalTok{    COSDictionary perms }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{COSDictionary}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    perms}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"DocMDP"}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{            signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCOSObject}\OperatorTok{());} 
\NormalTok{    doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getCOSObject}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setItem}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Perms"}\OperatorTok{),}\NormalTok{ perms}\OperatorTok{);}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  The \texttt{/P} value is the permission level: 1, 2, or 3.
\item
  The \texttt{/Perms} dictionary in the document catalog must reference
  the certification signature's \texttt{/Sig} dictionary. This is how
  viewers discover that the document has been certified.
\end{itemize}

A document can have at most \emph{one} certification signature, and it
must be the first signature applied. If you try to add a certification
signature to a document that already has one, the existing certification
is invalidated. If you apply it as a second signature (after an approval
signature), the result is undefined --- some viewers accept it, some
reject it, and Adobe Acrobat shows a confusing error.

\section{PDFBox 2.x to 3.x
Migration}\label{_pdfbox_2_x_to_3_x_migration}

PDFBox 3.0.0 introduced breaking API changes that affect every piece of
signing code. If you are migrating from PDFBox 2.x (which many projects
still use), these are the changes you need to know.

\subsection{Document Loading}\label{_document_loading}

PDFBox 2.x (removed)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// PDFBox 2.x {-}{-} these methods no longer exist}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDDocument}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{File}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"doc.pdf"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDDocument}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{inputStream}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDDocument}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteArray}\OperatorTok{);}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

PDFBox 3.x (current)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// PDFBox 3.x {-}{-} use the Loader class}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{File}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"doc.pdf"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteArray}\OperatorTok{);}
\CommentTok{// No direct InputStream loading {-}{-} read to byte[] first}
\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ bytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ inputStream}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{readAllBytes}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{bytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{Loader} class is in \texttt{org.apache.pdfbox.Loader}. The
old \texttt{PDDocument.load()} static methods were removed
entirely --- not deprecated, \emph{removed}.

\subsection{Standard 14 Fonts}\label{_standard_14_fonts}

This is the single most common compilation error in PDFBox 2.x-to-3.x
migration.

PDFBox 2.x (removed)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// PDFBox 2.x {-}{-} static font constants}
\NormalTok{PDFont font }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{PDFont fontBold }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA\_BOLD}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{PDFont fontMono }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COURIER}\OperatorTok{;}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

PDFBox 3.x (current)

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// PDFBox 3.x {-}{-} constructor with FontName enum}
\NormalTok{PDFont font }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{PDFont fontBold }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA\_BOLD}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{PDFont fontMono }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{COURIER}\OperatorTok{);}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{Standard14Fonts.FontName} enum lives in
\texttt{org.apache.pdfbox.pdmodel.font.Standard14Fonts}.

\subsection{AcroForm API Changes}\label{_acroform_api_changes}

PDFBox 2.x

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ needsAppearances }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ acroForm}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isNeedAppearances}\OperatorTok{();} \CommentTok{// gone}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

PDFBox 3.x

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ needsAppearances }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ acroForm}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNeedAppearances}\OperatorTok{();} 
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  The method was renamed from \texttt{isNeedAppearances()} to
  \texttt{getNeedAppearances()}. A small change, but it breaks at
  compile time.
\end{itemize}

\subsection{Content Stream Writing}\label{_content_stream_writing}

PDFBox 2.x --- could use PDPageContentStream for Form XObjects

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// This approach no longer works for Form XObjects in 3.x}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

PDFBox 3.x --- use the stream's output stream directly

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{PDFormXObject form }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDFormXObject}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{);}
\ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{OutputStream}\NormalTok{ os }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getStream}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{createOutputStream}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    os}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{write}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{contentStreamBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Complete Migration
Checklist}\label{_complete_migration_checklist}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3636}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3636}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2727}}@{}}
\caption{PDFBox 2.x to 3.x Breaking Changes Relevant to
Signing}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
PDFBox 2.x
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
PDFBox 3.x
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Impact
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
PDFBox 2.x
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
PDFBox 3.x
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Impact
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
\texttt{PDDocument.load(File)} & \texttt{Loader.loadPDF(File)} & Compile
error \\
\texttt{PDDocument.load(byte{[}{]})} &
\texttt{Loader.loadPDF(byte{[}{]})} & Compile error \\
\texttt{PDDocument.load(InputStream)} & \texttt{Loader.loadPDF(bytes)}
(read first) & Compile error \\
\texttt{PDType1Font.HELVETICA} &
\texttt{new\ PDType1Font(Standard14Fonts.FontName.HELVETICA)} & Compile
error \\
\texttt{acroForm.isNeedAppearances()} &
\texttt{acroForm.getNeedAppearances()} & Compile error \\
\texttt{new\ PDPageContentStream(doc,\ formXObject)} &
\texttt{form.getStream().createOutputStream()} & Compile error \\
\texttt{PDFont.getEncoding()} & Not available --- use COS dictionary &
Compile error \\
\texttt{PDFont.getToUnicodeCMap()} & Not visible --- check COS key &
Compile error \\
\end{longtable}

When migrating a large codebase, search for every occurrence of
\texttt{PDDocument.load}, \texttt{PDType1Font.HELVETICA} (and all other
font constants), and \texttt{isNeedAppearances}. These three account for
about 90\% of compilation failures in signing code.

\section{Common Pitfalls}\label{_common_pitfalls_3}

We have collected these from production incidents, Stack Overflow
questions, and our own debugging sessions. Each one has cost someone
hours.

\subsection{Pitfall 1: Not Registering the BouncyCastle
Provider}\label{_pitfall_1_not_registering_the_bouncycastle_provider}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{// This line MUST execute before any signing operation}
\NormalTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{Security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addProvider}\OperatorTok{(}
        \KeywordTok{new}\NormalTok{ org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{jce}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{provider}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BouncyCastleProvider}\OperatorTok{());}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Without this, you get a \texttt{NoSuchAlgorithmException} or a
\texttt{NoSuchProviderException} at runtime. The error message varies
depending on which BouncyCastle class triggers first, making it
confusing to diagnose. In Spring Boot applications, put this in a
\texttt{@PostConstruct} method of a \texttt{@Configuration} class:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{jakarta}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{annotation}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PostConstruct}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{provider}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{BouncyCastleProvider}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{springframework}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{context}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{annotation}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Configuration}\OperatorTok{;}

\AttributeTok{@Configuration}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ BouncyCastleConfig }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \AttributeTok{@PostConstruct}
    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{registerProvider}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{Security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{Security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addProvider}\OperatorTok{(}
