            \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{,}  \CommentTok{// no CRLs in the CMS itself (they go in the DSS)}
            \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{DERSet}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerInfo}\OperatorTok{));}

    \CommentTok{// {-}{-}{-} 5. Wrap in ContentInfo {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{    ContentInfo cmsContentInfo }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{ContentInfo}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            CMSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{signedData}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            signedData}\OperatorTok{);}

    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ cmsContentInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getEncoded}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"DER"}\OperatorTok{);}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Algorithm Detection for CSC
Credentials}\label{_algorithm_detection_for_csc_credentials}

The CSC credential info response tells you the key algorithm (RSA or EC)
but not always the exact signing algorithm OID. Use the key algorithm
OID to determine the correct signing algorithm:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Determines the signing algorithm OID from the CSC credential}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ key algorithm}\CommentTok{. }\NormalTok{Defaults to SHA256withRSA for RSA keys and}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ SHA256withECDSA for EC keys}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{resolveSignAlgoOid}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ keyAlgorithmOid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{keyAlgorithmOid }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}
            \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ keyAlgorithmOid}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{startsWith}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"1.2.840.10045"}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// EC key — use SHA256withECDSA}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \StringTok{"1.2.840.10045.4.3.2"}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
    \CommentTok{// RSA key — use SHA256withRSA (PKCS\#1 v1.5)}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.11"}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Adding an Unsigned Timestamp to the Remote
CMS}\label{_adding_an_unsigned_timestamp_to_the_remote_cms}

After assembling the CMS with the remote signature value, you can add an
RFC 3161 timestamp as an unsigned attribute to achieve PAdES-B-T. This
requires parsing the CMS back, adding the unsigned attribute, and
re-encoding:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Adds an RFC }\CommentTok{3161}\NormalTok{ timestamp token as an unsigned attribute}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ to an existing CMS SignedData}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ The timestamp is over the signature value }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{not the content}\CommentTok{).}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{addTimestampToCms}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ cmsBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signatureValue}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ tsaUrl}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// 1. Get timestamp token over the signature value}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ tsToken }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{requestTimestamp}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signatureValue}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsaUrl}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// 2. Parse the existing CMS}
\NormalTok{    CMSSignedData signedData }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cmsBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformationStore signerStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signedData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformation signer }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}

    \CommentTok{// 3. Add the timestamp as an unsigned attribute}
    \BuiltInTok{Hashtable}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ASN1ObjectIdentifier}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ unsignedAttrs }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{Hashtable}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
\NormalTok{    unsignedAttrs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{put}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            PKCSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signatureTimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{,}
            \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    PKCSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signatureTimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{DERSet}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ASN1Primitive}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{fromByteArray}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsToken}\OperatorTok{))));}

\NormalTok{    AttributeTable unsignedTable }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{AttributeTable}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{unsignedAttrs}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformation timestampedSigner }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            SignerInformation}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{replaceUnsignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    signer}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ unsignedTable}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// 4. Rebuild the SignedData with the updated SignerInfo}
    \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{SignerInformation}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ newSigners }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
\NormalTok{    newSigners}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{timestampedSigner}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    CMSSignedData timestampedCms }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{replaceSigners}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            signedData}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignerInformationStore}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{newSigners}\OperatorTok{));}

    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ timestampedCms}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getEncoded}\OperatorTok{();}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

This pattern --- build CMS without unsigned attributes, then parse and
add the timestamp --- is the standard approach for remote signing. You
cannot add the timestamp during initial CMS construction because the
timestamp must cover the signature value, which does not exist until the
CSC API returns it. The two-phase assembly is inherent to the remote
signing model.

For local signing (where you have the private key), BouncyCastle's
\texttt{CMSSignedDataGenerator} can add the timestamp in a single pass.
The manual assembly shown here is only needed when the signing operation
is split between local CMS construction and remote cryptographic
operation.

\section{Putting It All Together}\label{_putting_it_all_together}

Here is how you use the PAdES-B-B and PAdES-B-T builders with PDFBox:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Loader}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDDocument}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{// {-}{-}{-} PAdES{-}B{-}B (no timestamp) {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{SignatureInterface bbSigner }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PadesBBBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{        privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{);}

\ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    PDSignature sig }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{    SignatureOptions opts }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    opts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{32768}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{    doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sig}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ bbSigner}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ opts}\OperatorTok{);}

    \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ out }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncremental}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{out}\OperatorTok{);}
    \CommentTok{// out.toByteArray() is the PAdES{-}B{-}B signed PDF}
\OperatorTok{\}}

\CommentTok{// {-}{-}{-} PAdES{-}B{-}T (with timestamp) {-}{-}{-}}
\NormalTok{SignatureInterface btSigner }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PadesBTBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{        privateKey}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certificate}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ chain}\OperatorTok{,}
        \StringTok{"http://timestamp.example.com/tsa"}\OperatorTok{);}

\ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    PDSignature sig }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{    sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{    SignatureOptions opts }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{SignatureOptions}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    opts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPreferredSignatureSize}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{65536}\OperatorTok{);} \CommentTok{// larger for timestamp}

\NormalTok{    doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sig}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ btSigner}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ opts}\OperatorTok{);}

    \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ out }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncremental}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{out}\OperatorTok{);}
    \CommentTok{// out.toByteArray() is the PAdES{-}B{-}T signed PDF}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{PadesBBBuilder} and \texttt{PadesBTBuilder} classes are
production-ready. They handle algorithm negotiation, ESS attribute
construction, certificate embedding, and timestamp integration. Drop
them into your project alongside the BouncyCastle and PDFBox
dependencies from Chapter 28, register the BouncyCastle provider, and
you have a complete signing pipeline.

In Chapter 31, we reverse the process: given a signed PDF, we verify the
CMS signature, validate the certificate chain, check the timestamp, and
extract every piece of information that these structures contain.

\chapter{Verification \& Analysis with
PDFBox}\label{_verification_analysis_with_pdfbox}

Signing a PDF is a matter of following a recipe. Verifying a signed PDF
is where things get interesting --- and where most implementations get
things wrong. PDFBox gives you every primitive you need: signature
extraction, byte range access, CMS parsing, content stream reading. But
it deliberately does not give you a "verify this signature" method. You
build the pipeline yourself, step by step, and you understand every
failure mode because you wrote the code that handles it.

This chapter walks through a complete verification implementation using
Apache PDFBox 3.0.0 and BouncyCastle 1.80. We start with extracting
signatures from a PDF document, proceed through each verification step
from Chapter 16, and end with a production-ready five-step pipeline that
handles the edge cases real-world PDFs throw at you. If Chapter 16
described \emph{what} to verify, this chapter shows you \emph{how} ---
with code you can compile and run.

\section{Extracting Signatures from a PDF
Document}\label{_extracting_signatures_from_a_pdf_document}

\subsection{The Extraction Path: AcroForm to
PDSignature}\label{_the_extraction_path_acroform_to_pdsignature}

Every PDF signature lives inside the AcroForm field tree. The extraction
path follows the object hierarchy we dissected in Chapter 12: the
document's \texttt{/AcroForm} dictionary contains a \texttt{/Fields}
array, which includes \texttt{PDSignatureField} entries, each of which
references a \texttt{PDSignature} dictionary.

PDFBox models this hierarchy directly. The key classes are:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \texttt{PDAcroForm} --- the form dictionary, accessed via
  \texttt{PDDocument.getDocumentCatalog().getAcroForm()}
\item
  \texttt{PDSignatureField} --- a form field of type \texttt{/Sig},
  extends \texttt{PDTerminalField}
\item
  \texttt{PDSignature} --- the actual \texttt{/Type\ /Sig} dictionary
  containing \texttt{/Contents}, \texttt{/ByteRange},
  \texttt{/SubFilter}, etc.
\end{itemize}

There are two ways to access signatures in PDFBox. The first walks the
field tree:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Extract all signature fields from the AcroForm field tree}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ This approach gives you access to the field }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{widget}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ appearance}\CommentTok{)}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ in addition to the signature dictionary}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{extractSignatureFields}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument document}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    PDAcroForm acroForm }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ document}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAcroForm}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{acroForm }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Document has no AcroForm — no signatures possible"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \BuiltInTok{Collections}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{emptyList}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ signatureFields }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDField field }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ acroForm}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getFields}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \FunctionTok{collectSignatureFields}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{field}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signatureFields}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Found \{\} signature field(s) in AcroForm"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signatureFields}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ signatureFields}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}

\KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{collectSignatureFields}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDField field}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ result}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{field }\KeywordTok{instanceof}\NormalTok{ PDSignatureField sigField}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        result}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sigField}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{field }\KeywordTok{instanceof}\NormalTok{ PDNonTerminalField nonTerminal}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDField child }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ nonTerminal}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getChildren}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \FunctionTok{collectSignatureFields}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{child}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ result}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The second approach bypasses the field tree and goes directly to the
signature dictionaries:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Extract all PDSignature dictionaries directly}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Faster}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ but you lose access to the field and widget objects}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Useful when you only need the cryptographic data}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{extractSignatures}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument document}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ signatures }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ document}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignatureDictionaries}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Found \{\} signature dictionaries"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signatures}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ signatures}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Use the first approach (field tree walk) when you need the full
picture --- field name, widget annotation, visual appearance, page
location. Use the second approach when you only need the cryptographic
payload --- for pure verification, it is faster and simpler.

\subsection{Reading Signature
Metadata}\label{_reading_signature_metadata}

Once you have a \texttt{PDSignature}, you can extract the metadata that
drives the verification pipeline:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{logSignatureMetadata}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature sig}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"SubFilter: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubFilter}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Filter: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getFilter}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Name: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getName}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Location: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getLocation}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Reason: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getReason}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Sign date: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignDate}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Contact info: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getContactInfo}\OperatorTok{());}

    \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ sig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getByteRange}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length} \OperatorTok{==} \DecValTok{4}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange: [\{\} \{\} \{\} \{\}]"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]);}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Signed bytes: \{\} + \{\} = \{\} total"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{],}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]);}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Gap (Contents): offset \{\} to \{\}, size \{\} bytes"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{],}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{/SubFilter} value tells you what kind of signature you are
dealing with:

\begin{itemize}
\item
  \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached} --- Classic PKCS\#7 (CMS) detached
  signature
\item
  \texttt{ETSI.CAdES.detached} --- PAdES CAdES-based signature (the
  modern standard)
\item
  \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.sha1} --- Legacy: SHA-1 hash embedded in PKCS\#7
  (deprecated, avoid)
\item
  \texttt{adbe.x509.rsa\_sha1} --- Legacy: bare RSA signature with X.509
  cert (deprecated, avoid)
\end{itemize}

For any new code, you should handle \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached} and
\texttt{ETSI.CAdES.detached}. The legacy formats exist in old documents,
and you may encounter them, but you should never produce them.

\section{Parsing the CMS SignedData from
/Contents}\label{_parsing_the_cms_signeddata_from_contents}

\subsection{Extracting the Raw CMS
Bytes}\label{_extracting_the_raw_cms_bytes}

The \texttt{/Contents} value in the signature dictionary is a
hex-encoded byte string containing the DER-encoded CMS
\texttt{SignedData} structure. PDFBox provides this as a byte array:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ cmsBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getContents}\OperatorTok{();}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

This single line hides a meaningful amount of work. PDFBox reads the hex
string from the PDF (which can be 16KB to 64KB or more of hex
characters), decodes it from hex to binary, and strips any trailing zero
padding. The result is the raw DER-encoded CMS \texttt{SignedData} that
BouncyCastle can parse.

\subsection{Parsing with BouncyCastle}\label{_parsing_with_bouncycastle}

To parse the CMS structure, you also need the signed bytes --- the byte
range segments from the PDF file. For a detached signature (which is
what \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached} and \texttt{ETSI.CAdES.detached} are),
the signed content is not inside the CMS; it is the external byte range
data.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Parse a CMS SignedData from the }\CommentTok{/}\NormalTok{Contents of a PDF signature}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{pdfBytes}\CommentTok{      }\NormalTok{the complete PDF file as a byte array}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{signature}\CommentTok{     }\NormalTok{the PDSignature whose CMS we want to parse}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ the parsed CMSSignedData}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ ready for verification}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ CMSSignedData }\FunctionTok{parseCmsSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature signature}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws}\NormalTok{ CMSException }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// Extract the signed byte ranges}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getByteRange}\OperatorTok{();}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signedContent }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[}\NormalTok{byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]];}
    \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{arraycopy}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ signedContent}\OperatorTok{,}
            \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]);}
    \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{arraycopy}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ signedContent}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]);}

    \CommentTok{// Parse the CMS with the signed content as the detached data}
\NormalTok{    CMSProcessableByteArray processable }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSProcessableByteArray}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedContent}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{processable}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getContents}\OperatorTok{());}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

A common mistake is to construct \texttt{CMSSignedData} with only the
\texttt{/Contents} bytes (without the signed content). This will parse
the CMS structure, but any subsequent verification call will fail
because BouncyCastle cannot compute the message digest without the
signed data. Always pass both the signed content and the CMS bytes.

\subsection{Navigating the CMS
Structure}\label{_navigating_the_cms_structure}

Once parsed, the \texttt{CMSSignedData} object exposes the full CMS tree
from RFC 5652 --- Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS):

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{inspectCmsStructure}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{CMSSignedData cmsData}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \CommentTok{// Signer information (usually exactly one for PDF signatures)}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformationStore signerStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Number of signers: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{SignerInformation signer }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ signerStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Digest algorithm OID: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signer}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDigestAlgOID}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Encryption algorithm OID: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                signer}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getEncryptionAlgOID}\OperatorTok{());}

        \CommentTok{// Signed attributes (authenticated)}
\NormalTok{        AttributeTable signedAttrs }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signer}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedAttrs }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Signed attributes count: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    signedAttrs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
            \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedAttrs}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ CMSAttributes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{contentType}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"contentType"}\OperatorTok{);}
            \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedAttrs}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ CMSAttributes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{messageDigest}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"messageDigest"}\OperatorTok{);}
            \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedAttrs}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ CMSAttributes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{signingTime}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"signingTime"}\OperatorTok{);}
            \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedAttrs}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    PKCSObjectIdentifiers}
                            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signingCertificateV2}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"ESS{-}signing{-}certificate{-}v2"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Unsigned attributes (unauthenticated — timestamps live here)}
\NormalTok{        AttributeTable unsignedAttrs }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signer}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getUnsignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{unsignedAttrs }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Unsigned attributes count: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    unsignedAttrs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
            \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{unsignedAttrs}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    PKCSObjectIdentifiers}
                            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signatureTimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"signatureTimeStampToken"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Embedded certificates}
\NormalTok{    Store}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ certStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{();}
    \BuiltInTok{Collection}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ certs }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            certStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Embedded certificates: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ certs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder cert }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ certs}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  Subject: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubject}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  Issuer: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getIssuer}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  Serial: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSerialNumber}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  Valid: \{\} to \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNotBefore}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNotAfter}\OperatorTok{());}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}

\KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{logAttribute}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{AttributeTable table}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        ASN1ObjectIdentifier oid}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ name}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\NormalTok{ attr }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ table}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{oid}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{attr }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  \{\} present, values: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                name}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ attr}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAttrValues}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Byte Range Verification in
Code}\label{_byte_range_verification_in_code}

\subsection{The Three Checks}\label{_the_three_checks}

Byte range verification (Step 1 from Chapter 16) requires three checks:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  The \texttt{/ByteRange} array has exactly four elements
\item
  The first offset is zero (the signed region starts at the beginning of
  the file)
\item
  The second segment extends to the end of the file (or the end of the
  revision for multi-signature documents)
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Verify the structural integrity of the byte range}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{byteRange}\CommentTok{   }\NormalTok{the four}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{element ByteRange array}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{fileSize}\CommentTok{    }\NormalTok{the total size of the PDF file in bytes}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ a ValidationResult indicating success or the specific failure}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult }\FunctionTok{verifyByteRange}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{long}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{||}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length} \OperatorTok{!=} \DecValTok{4}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange is null or does not have exactly 4 elements"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_MALFORMED}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ offset1 }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ length1 }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ offset2 }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ length2 }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{];}

    \CommentTok{// Check 1: First segment starts at byte 0}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{offset1 }\OperatorTok{!=} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange offset1 is \{\} — expected 0"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ offset1}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_NOT\_AT\_START}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Check 2: Segments don\textquotesingle{}t overlap}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{offset2 }\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ length1}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange segments overlap: "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"segment1 ends at \{\}, segment2 starts at \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                length1}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ offset2}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_OVERLAP}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Check 3: Signed region extends to end of file}
    \DataTypeTok{long}\NormalTok{ signedEnd }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{long}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ offset2 }\OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ length2}\OperatorTok{;}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedEnd }\OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \DataTypeTok{long}\NormalTok{ trailingBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ fileSize }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ signedEnd}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange covers \{\} bytes but file is \{\} bytes — "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"\{\} trailing unsigned bytes detected"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                signedEnd}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ trailingBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_TRAILING\_BYTES}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange verified: [\{\} \{\} \{\} \{\}], "} \OperatorTok{+}
            \StringTok{"file size \{\}, no trailing bytes"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            offset1}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ length1}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ offset2}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ length2}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Trailing bytes are not always an attack. In a multi-signature document,
each signature covers only up to the end of its own incremental
revision. Subsequent revisions (containing later signatures) appear as
"trailing bytes" from the perspective of the earlier signature. Chapter
8 covers how incremental updates work, and Chapter 13 covers the byte
range geometry in multi-signature scenarios.

However, trailing bytes on the \emph{last} signature in a document are
suspicious and may indicate a Shadow Attack (Chapter 24). Your
verification code should distinguish between "trailing bytes that are a
legitimate later revision" and "trailing bytes that are unexplained."

\subsection{Validating the Contents
Gap}\label{_validating_the_contents_gap}

The gap between the two segments should contain exactly the
\texttt{/Contents} hex string. You can validate this by checking that
the gap bytes, when decoded from hex, produce valid DER-encoded data:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Validate that the gap between byte range segments contains}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ a properly formatted hex string with valid CMS data}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{validateContentsGap}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ gapStart }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ gapEnd }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ gapSize }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ gapEnd }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ gapStart}\OperatorTok{;}

    \CommentTok{// The gap should start with \textquotesingle{}\textless{}\textquotesingle{} and end with \textquotesingle{}\textgreater{}\textquotesingle{}}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{[}\NormalTok{gapStart}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{!=} \CharTok{\textquotesingle{}\textless{}\textquotesingle{}} \OperatorTok{||}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{[}\NormalTok{gapEnd }\OperatorTok{{-}} \DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{!=} \CharTok{\textquotesingle{}\textgreater{}\textquotesingle{}}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Gap does not start with \textquotesingle{}\textless{}\textquotesingle{} or end with \textquotesingle{}\textgreater{}\textquotesingle{}"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Extract hex content (skip the delimiters)}
    \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ hexContent }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{String}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ gapStart }\OperatorTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            gapSize }\OperatorTok{{-}} \DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ StandardCharsets}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{US\_ASCII}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{trim}\OperatorTok{();}

    \CommentTok{// Verify it\textquotesingle{}s valid hex}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(!}\NormalTok{hexContent}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{matches}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"[0{-}9a{-}fA{-}F]+"}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Gap contains non{-}hex characters"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Contents gap validated: \{\} hex chars = \{\} bytes CMS"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            hexContent}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ hexContent}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{/} \DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{CMS Cryptographic
Verification}\label{_cms_cryptographic_verification}

\subsection{Building the
SignerInformationVerifier}\label{_building_the_signerinformationverifier}

This is Step 3 from Chapter 16 --- verifying that the CMS
\texttt{SignedData} was actually created by the claimed signer's private
key. BouncyCastle's \texttt{SignerInformation.verify()} method performs
this check, but you need to provide the correct
\texttt{SignerInformationVerifier}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Verify the CMS signature cryptographically}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{cmsData}\CommentTok{  }\NormalTok{the parsed CMS SignedData }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{with signed content}\CommentTok{)}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ the signer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate if verification succeeds}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ null otherwise}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ X509CertificateHolder }\FunctionTok{verifyCmsSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{CMSSignedData cmsData}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{    SignerInformationStore signerStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{==} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"CMS contains no signers"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    SignerInformation signerInfo }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            signerStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}

    \CommentTok{// Find the signer\textquotesingle{}s certificate by matching the SignerIdentifier}
\NormalTok{    Store}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ certStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{();}
    \AttributeTok{@SuppressWarnings}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"unchecked"}\OperatorTok{)}
    \BuiltInTok{Collection}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ matches }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            certStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSID}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{matches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEmpty}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"No certificate matches the SignerIdentifier — "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"the signer\textquotesingle{}s cert may not be embedded in the CMS"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    X509CertificateHolder signerCert }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ matches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Signer certificate: subject=\{\}, issuer=\{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            signerCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubject}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ signerCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getIssuer}\OperatorTok{());}

    \CommentTok{// Build the verifier and verify}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformationVerifier verifier }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaSimpleSignerInfoVerifierBuilder}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert}\OperatorTok{);}

    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ valid }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{verify}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{verifier}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{valid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"CMS signature verification PASSED"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ signerCert}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"CMS signature verification FAILED — "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"signature was not created by the claimed certificate"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{What verify() Actually
Does}\label{_what_verify_actually_does}

When you call \texttt{signerInfo.verify(verifier)}, BouncyCastle
performs the following internally:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  Computes the digest of the signed content (the byte range data you
  passed when constructing \texttt{CMSSignedData}) using the algorithm
  from \texttt{SignerInfo.digestAlgorithm}
\item
  Checks that this computed digest matches the \texttt{messageDigest}
  attribute in the signed attributes
\item
  DER-encodes the signed attributes
\item
  Verifies the RSA/ECDSA/DSA signature over the DER-encoded signed
  attributes using the public key from the signer's certificate
\end{enumerate}

If signed attributes are absent (which is technically allowed by CMS but
rare in PDF signatures), BouncyCastle instead verifies the signature
directly over the content digest.

The \texttt{verify()} method performs only the cryptographic
verification --- Steps 2 and 3 combined. It does not check certificate
validity, revocation status, or trust. Those are separate steps you must
implement yourself.

\section{Certificate Chain Reconstruction and
Validation}\label{_certificate_chain_reconstruction_and_validation}

\subsection{The Java PKIX CertPath
API}\label{_the_java_pkix_certpath_api}

Java's \texttt{java.security.cert} package provides a complete PKIX
certificate path validation engine via \texttt{CertPathBuilder} and
\texttt{CertPathValidator}. This is the standard way to validate
certificate chains in Java, and it handles the complexity of chain
building, validity checking, basic constraints verification, and key
usage checking.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Build and validate the certificate chain from the signer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ to a trusted root}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{signerCert}\CommentTok{    }\NormalTok{the signer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{from CMS verification}\CommentTok{)}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{cmsData}\CommentTok{       }\NormalTok{the CMS containing intermediate certificates}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{trustAnchors}\CommentTok{  }\NormalTok{the set of trusted root certificates}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{signingTime}\CommentTok{   }\NormalTok{the claimed signing time }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{for validity checks}\CommentTok{)}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ the validated CertPath}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ or null if validation fails}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{CertPath} \FunctionTok{validateCertificateChain}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{        X509CertificateHolder signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        CMSSignedData cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ signingTime}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// Convert BouncyCastle cert holders to Java X509Certificates}
    \BuiltInTok{CertificateFactory}\NormalTok{ cf }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{CertificateFactory}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"X.509"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    JcaX509CertificateConverter converter }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaX509CertificateConverter}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// Collect all certificates from CMS into a CertStore}
    \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ allCerts }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
\NormalTok{    Store}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ bcStore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder holder }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ bcStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        allCerts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{converter}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{holder}\OperatorTok{));}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \BuiltInTok{CertStore}\NormalTok{ certStore }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{CertStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Collection"}\OperatorTok{,}
            \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{CollectionCertStoreParameters}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{allCerts}\OperatorTok{));}

    \CommentTok{// Configure the target certificate selector}
    \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ targetCert }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ converter}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert}\OperatorTok{);}
    \BuiltInTok{X509CertSelector}\NormalTok{ targetSelector }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{X509CertSelector}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    targetSelector}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{targetCert}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// Build PKIX parameters}
    \BuiltInTok{PKIXBuilderParameters}\NormalTok{ params }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{PKIXBuilderParameters}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ targetSelector}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addCertStore}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{certStore}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setRevocationEnabled}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{);} \CommentTok{// We handle revocation separately}
\NormalTok{    params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setDate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signingTime}\OperatorTok{);}        \CommentTok{// Validate at signing time}

    \CommentTok{// Build the chain}
    \BuiltInTok{CertPathBuilder}\NormalTok{ builder }\OperatorTok{=}
            \BuiltInTok{CertPathBuilder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"PKIX"}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \BuiltInTok{PKIXCertPathBuilderResult}\NormalTok{ result }\OperatorTok{=}
            \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{PKIXCertPathBuilderResult}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ builder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{params}\OperatorTok{);}

    \BuiltInTok{CertPath}\NormalTok{ certPath }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ result}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertPath}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate chain built successfully — \{\} certificates"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            certPath}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ i }\OperatorTok{=} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{;}\NormalTok{ i }\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{ certPath}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{();}\NormalTok{ i}\OperatorTok{++)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ cert }\OperatorTok{=}
                \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ certPath}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{i}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  [\{\}] Subject: \{\}, Issuer: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                i}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getIssuerX500Principal}\OperatorTok{());}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"  [root] Trust anchor: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            result}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getTrustedCert}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ certPath}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Building the Trust Anchor
Set}\label{_building_the_trust_anchor_set}

The \texttt{TrustAnchor} set represents your trust store --- the root
certificates you are willing to trust. In a real application, this might
come from the Java default trust store (\texttt{cacerts}), a custom
keystore, or the AATL/EUTL (see Chapter 16, Step 5).

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Load trust anchors from a Java KeyStore }\CommentTok{(}\NormalTok{JKS or PKCS12}\CommentTok{).}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{loadTrustAnchors}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Path keystorePath}\OperatorTok{,}
        \DataTypeTok{char}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\NormalTok{ trustStore }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            keystorePath}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toString}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{endsWith}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{".p12"}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{?} \StringTok{"PKCS12"} \OperatorTok{:} \StringTok{"JKS"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ is }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Files}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{newInputStream}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{keystorePath}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        trustStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{is}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ anchors }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{HashSet}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
    \BuiltInTok{Enumeration}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{String}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ aliases }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ trustStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{aliases}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{while} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{aliases}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{hasMoreElements}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ alias }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ aliases}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{nextElement}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{trustStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isCertificateEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alias}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ cert }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ trustStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{alias}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            anchors}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cert}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded trust anchor: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{());}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Loaded \{\} trust anchors from \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ anchors}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{            keystorePath}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ anchors}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Key Usage Validation}\label{_key_usage_validation}

The PKIX \texttt{CertPathValidator} checks basic constraints and key
usage automatically. But if you want to verify specific requirements ---
for example, that the signer's certificate has the
\texttt{digitalSignature} or \texttt{nonRepudiation} key usage --- you
can check explicitly:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Check that the signer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate permits digital signing}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{hasSigningKeyUsage}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ keyUsage }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getKeyUsage}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{keyUsage }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate has no KeyUsage extension — "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"signing permitted by default"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// bit 0 = digitalSignature, bit 1 = nonRepudiation (contentCommitment)}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ digitalSignature }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ keyUsage}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{];}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ nonRepudiation }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ keyUsage}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{];}

    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(!}\NormalTok{digitalSignature }\OperatorTok{\&\&} \OperatorTok{!}\NormalTok{nonRepudiation}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate KeyUsage does not include "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"digitalSignature or nonRepudiation"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate KeyUsage: digitalSignature=\{\}, "} \OperatorTok{+}
            \StringTok{"nonRepudiation=\{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ digitalSignature}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ nonRepudiation}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Timestamp Verification}\label{_timestamp_verification_2}

\subsection{Extracting the Timestamp
Token}\label{_extracting_the_timestamp_token}

A signature timestamp is an unsigned CMS attribute with OID
\texttt{1.2.840.113549.1.9.16.2.14}
(\texttt{id-aa-signatureTimeStampToken}). The attribute value is itself
a CMS \texttt{SignedData} structure --- a timestamp is a signature on a
signature:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Extract and verify the signature timestamp}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ if present}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{signerInfo}\CommentTok{  }\NormalTok{the CMS SignerInformation}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ the verified signing time}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ or null if no timestamp or invalid}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{Date} \FunctionTok{verifyTimestamp}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{SignerInformation signerInfo}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{    AttributeTable unsignedAttrs }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getUnsignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{();}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{unsignedAttrs }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"No unsigned attributes — no timestamp present"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\NormalTok{ tsAttr }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ unsignedAttrs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            PKCSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signatureTimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsAttr }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"No signatureTimeStampToken attribute found"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Parse the timestamp token}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ tsBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsAttr}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAttrValues}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getObjectAt}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{toASN1Primitive}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getEncoded}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    CMSSignedData tsSignedData }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    TimeStampToken tsToken }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{TimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsSignedData}\OperatorTok{);}

    \CommentTok{// Verify the timestamp\textquotesingle{}s CMS signature}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformation tsSigner }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsSignedData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}
    \AttributeTok{@SuppressWarnings}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"unchecked"}\OperatorTok{)}
    \BuiltInTok{Collection}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ tsMatches }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            tsSignedData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsSigner}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSID}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsMatches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEmpty}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp token does not contain "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"the TSA\textquotesingle{}s certificate"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    X509CertificateHolder tsCert }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsMatches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}

    \CommentTok{// Verify the TSA certificate has timeStamping EKU}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ hasTsEku }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{;}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getExtensions}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        Extension ekuExt }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getExtensions}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getExtension}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                Extension}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{extendedKeyUsage}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ekuExt }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            ExtendedKeyUsage eku }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                    ExtendedKeyUsage}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ekuExt}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getParsedValue}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{            hasTsEku }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ eku}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{hasKeyPurposeId}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    KeyPurposeId}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_kp\_timeStamping}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(!}\NormalTok{hasTsEku}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"TSA certificate does not have "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"id{-}kp{-}timeStamping EKU — "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"timestamp may not be trusted"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Verify the timestamp\textquotesingle{}s CMS cryptographic signature}
\NormalTok{    SignerInformationVerifier tsVerifier }\OperatorTok{=}
            \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaSimpleSignerInfoVerifierBuilder}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsCert}\OperatorTok{);}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ tsValid }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsSigner}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{verify}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsVerifier}\OperatorTok{);}

    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(!}\NormalTok{tsValid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp CMS signature verification FAILED"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ signingTime }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsToken}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getGenTime}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp verified — signing time: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signingTime}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"TSA: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubject}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp serial: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            tsToken}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getSerialNumber}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp hash algorithm: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            tsToken}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getHashAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getId}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ signingTime}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Verifying the Timestamp Covers the
Signature}\label{_verifying_the_timestamp_covers_the_signature}

A valid timestamp is not just a signed time --- it must also prove that
the \emph{signature value} existed at that time. The timestamp's message
imprint digest must match the hash of the signature value from the
\texttt{SignerInfo}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Verify that the timestamp}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s message imprint matches the signature value}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{verifyTimestampCoversSignature}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{        TimeStampToken tsToken}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        SignerInformation signerInfo}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{// The timestamp\textquotesingle{}s imprint should be the hash of the signature value}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signatureBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
    \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ tsHashAlg }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsToken}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{()}
            \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getHashAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getId}\OperatorTok{();}
    \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ algName }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{mapOidToAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsHashAlg}\OperatorTok{);}

    \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\NormalTok{ digest }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{algName}\OperatorTok{);}
    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ computedImprint }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ digest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{digest}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signatureBytes}\OperatorTok{);}

    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ claimedImprint }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            tsToken}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getMessageImprintDigest}\OperatorTok{();}

    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ match }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEqual}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{computedImprint}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ claimedImprint}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{match}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp message imprint matches signature value"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp message imprint does NOT match "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"signature value — timestamp may cover different data"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ match}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{The Complete Five-Step Verification
Pipeline}\label{_the_complete_five_step_verification_pipeline}

\subsection{Putting It All Together}\label{_putting_it_all_together_2}

Here is the complete verification pipeline that combines every step from
this chapter. Each step is independent --- a failure at one step does
not prevent the other steps from running. The pipeline produces a
comprehensive \texttt{VerificationReport} with per-step results:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Complete signature verification pipeline}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Runs all five verification steps and produces a comprehensive report}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ VerificationReport }\FunctionTok{verifySignature}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature signature}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{    VerificationReport report }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{VerificationReport}\OperatorTok{();}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// === Step 1: Byte Range Structural Integrity ===}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 1: Verifying byte range..."}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getByteRange}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        ValidationResult byteRangeResult }\OperatorTok{=}
                \FunctionTok{verifyByteRange}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setByteRangeResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRangeResult}\OperatorTok{);}

        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRangeResult }\OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ byteRangeResult}
                    \OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_TRAILING\_BYTES}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 1 FAILED: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRangeResult}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{;} \CommentTok{// Cannot proceed without valid byte range}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// === Step 2: Hash Verification ===}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 2: Verifying message digest..."}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        CMSSignedData cmsData }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{parseCmsSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        SignerInformation signerInfo }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}

        \CommentTok{// Compute the hash of the signed bytes}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ digestAlgOid }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDigestAlgOID}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ algName }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{mapOidToAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{digestAlgOid}\OperatorTok{);}
        \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\NormalTok{ md }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{algName}\OperatorTok{);}
