\NormalTok{        md}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{update}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]);}
\NormalTok{        md}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{update}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]);}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ computedHash }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ md}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{digest}\OperatorTok{();}

        \CommentTok{// Compare to the CMS messageDigest attribute}
        \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\NormalTok{ digestAttr }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{CMSAttributes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{messageDigest}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ claimedHash }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ ASN1OctetString}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                digestAttr}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAttrValues}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getObjectAt}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{)).}\FunctionTok{getOctets}\OperatorTok{();}

        \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ hashMatch }\OperatorTok{=}
                \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEqual}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{computedHash}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ claimedHash}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setHashResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{hashMatch}
                \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HASH\_MISMATCH}\OperatorTok{);}

        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(!}\NormalTok{hashMatch}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 2 FAILED: computed hash does not match "} \OperatorTok{+}
                    \StringTok{"CMS messageDigest — document was modified"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 2 PASSED: hash matches"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// === Step 3: CMS Cryptographic Verification ===}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 3: Verifying CMS signature..."}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        X509CertificateHolder signerCert }\OperatorTok{=}
                \FunctionTok{verifyCmsSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cmsData}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCmsResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}
                \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{CMS\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{);}

        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 3 FAILED: CMS verification failed"}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 3 PASSED: CMS signature valid"}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// === Step 4: Certificate Chain Validation ===}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 4: Validating certificate chain..."}\OperatorTok{);}
        \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ validationDate }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{Date}\OperatorTok{();} \CommentTok{// Default: now}
        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \CommentTok{// Check for timestamp first — use timestamp time if available}
            \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ tsTime }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{verifyTimestamp}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerInfo}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsTime }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                validationDate }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsTime}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{                report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTimestampTime}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsTime}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTimestampResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Using timestamp time for chain validation: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                        tsTime}\OperatorTok{);}
            \OperatorTok{\}}

            \BuiltInTok{CertPath}\NormalTok{ certPath }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{validateCertificateChain}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ validationDate}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setChainResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{certPath }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}
                    \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                    \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{CHAIN\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCertPath}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{certPath}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{catch} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{CertPathBuilderException}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 4 FAILED: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMessage}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setChainResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{CHAIN\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setChainError}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{e}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMessage}\OperatorTok{());}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// === Step 5: Trust Evaluation ===}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 5: Evaluating trust..."}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getChainResult}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTrustResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 5 PASSED: certificate chains to "} \OperatorTok{+}
                    \StringTok{"a trusted root"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTrustResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{NOT\_TRUSTED}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Step 5: trust could not be established"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

    \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{catch} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Exception}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Verification pipeline failed with exception"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setException}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{e}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Verification complete: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSummary}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{The VerificationReport
Model}\label{_the_verificationreport_model}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\AttributeTok{@Data}
\AttributeTok{@Builder}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ VerificationReport }\OperatorTok{\{}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult byteRangeResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult hashResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult cmsResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult chainResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult trustResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult timestampResult}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ timestampTime}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \BuiltInTok{CertPath}\NormalTok{ certPath}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ chainError}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \BuiltInTok{Exception}\NormalTok{ exception}\OperatorTok{;}

    \KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{getSummary}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{exception }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{return} \StringTok{"ERROR: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ exception}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMessage}\OperatorTok{();}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
        \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ allPassed }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                byteRangeResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ hashResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ cmsResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ chainResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ trustResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ allPassed }\OperatorTok{?} \StringTok{"ALL STEPS PASSED"} \OperatorTok{:} \StringTok{"ONE OR MORE STEPS FAILED"}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{isValid}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ exception }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ byteRangeResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ hashResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ cmsResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{isTrusted}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \FunctionTok{isValid}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ chainResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ trustResult }\OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{The ValidationResult Enum}\label{_the_validationresult_enum}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{enum}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult }\OperatorTok{\{}
    \FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Passed"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_MALFORMED}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange array is malformed"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_NOT\_AT\_START}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange does not start at offset 0"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_OVERLAP}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"ByteRange segments overlap"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_TRAILING\_BYTES}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Unsigned trailing bytes detected"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{HASH\_MISMATCH}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Computed hash does not match CMS messageDigest"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{CMS\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"CMS cryptographic verification failed"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{CHAIN\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate chain validation failed"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{NOT\_TRUSTED}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Signer certificate does not chain to a trusted root"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{TIMESTAMP\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Timestamp verification failed"}\OperatorTok{),}
    \FunctionTok{REVOKED}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"A certificate in the chain has been revoked"}\OperatorTok{);}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{final} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ description}\OperatorTok{;}

    \FunctionTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ description}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{description} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ description}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{getDescription}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ description}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Modification Detection Between
Revisions}\label{_modification_detection_between_revisions}

\subsection{Why Multi-Signature Documents Need Revision
Comparison}\label{_why_multi_signature_documents_need_revision_comparison}

When a PDF has multiple signatures, each signature covers a specific
revision of the document --- a snapshot of the file at the point when
that signature was applied. Later signatures add incremental updates
(Chapter 8) that extend the file. From the perspective of the first
signature, the second signature's bytes are "trailing bytes" beyond its
byte range.

The critical question is: \textbf{did the incremental update between
revisions only add the new signature, or did it modify existing
content?}

If someone added a new page, changed text, or altered form field values
between signing events, the earlier signature's byte range still
verifies (the signed bytes haven't changed), but the document's visible
content has changed. This is the basis of the Shadow Attacks described
in Chapter 24.

\subsection{Extracting Individual
Revisions}\label{_extracting_individual_revisions}

PDFBox allows you to extract the bytes of each revision by using the
byte range information:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Extract the signed revision for a specific signature}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Returns the PDF bytes up to the end of the signature}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s coverage}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{extractSignedRevision}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature signature}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getByteRange}\OperatorTok{();}
    \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ revisionEnd }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{];}

    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ revision }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[}\NormalTok{revisionEnd}\OperatorTok{];}
    \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{arraycopy}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ revision}\OperatorTok{,} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ revisionEnd}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Extracted revision: \{\} bytes (full file: \{\} bytes)"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            revisionEnd}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ revision}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Compare two revisions to detect modifications beyond signature additions}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Returns a list of differences found}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{RevisionDifference}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}} \FunctionTok{compareRevisions}\OperatorTok{(}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ earlierRevision}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ laterRevision}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{RevisionDifference}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ differences }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument earlier }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlierRevision}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{         PDDocument later }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{laterRevision}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}

        \CommentTok{// Check page count changes}
        \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ earlyPages }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ earlier}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNumberOfPages}\OperatorTok{();}
        \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ laterPages }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ later}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNumberOfPages}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlyPages }\OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ laterPages}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            differences}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{RevisionDifference}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    DifferenceType}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{PAGE\_COUNT\_CHANGED}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"Page count changed from "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ earlyPages }\OperatorTok{+}
                    \StringTok{" to "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ laterPages}\OperatorTok{));}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Check AcroForm field value changes}
\NormalTok{        PDAcroForm earlyForm }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                earlier}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAcroForm}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        PDAcroForm laterForm }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                later}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAcroForm}\OperatorTok{();}

        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlyForm }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ laterForm }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \FunctionTok{compareFormFields}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlyForm}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ laterForm}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ differences}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \CommentTok{// Check for new annotations}
        \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ i }\OperatorTok{=} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{                i }\OperatorTok{\textless{}} \BuiltInTok{Math}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{min}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlyPages}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ laterPages}\OperatorTok{);}\NormalTok{ i}\OperatorTok{++)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ earlyAnnots }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ earlier}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{i}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAnnotations}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{();}
            \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ laterAnnots }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ later}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{i}\OperatorTok{)}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAnnotations}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{();}
            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{earlyAnnots }\OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ laterAnnots}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                differences}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{RevisionDifference}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        DifferenceType}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{ANNOTATIONS\_CHANGED}\OperatorTok{,}
                        \StringTok{"Page "} \OperatorTok{+} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{i }\OperatorTok{+} \DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{": annotations changed "} \OperatorTok{+}
                        \StringTok{"from "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ earlyAnnots }\OperatorTok{+} \StringTok{" to "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ laterAnnots}\OperatorTok{));}
            \OperatorTok{\}}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Revision comparison found \{\} difference(s)"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            differences}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{size}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ differences}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

Revision comparison is inherently incomplete. A sophisticated attacker
can modify content streams in ways that are difficult to detect by
comparing PDF objects. The comparison above catches obvious changes
(page count, form fields, annotations), but it is not a security
guarantee. For high-security applications, consider using the EU DSS
framework, which implements the full ETSI modification detection
specification.

\section{Edge Cases: Self-Signed, Expired, and Missing
Chain}\label{_edge_cases_self_signed_expired_and_missing_chain}

\subsection{Self-Signed Certificates}\label{_self_signed_certificates_2}

A self-signed certificate is one where the issuer and subject are the
same entity --- the certificate signs itself. Self-signed signatures are
cryptographically valid (the CMS verifies), but the certificate chain is
trivially short: one certificate, no intermediates, no root CA.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Check if a certificate is self}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{signed}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{boolean} \FunctionTok{isSelfSigned}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder cert}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ selfSigned }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubject}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{equals}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getIssuer}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{selfSigned}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate is self{-}signed: subject and issuer "} \OperatorTok{+}
                \StringTok{"are both \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubject}\OperatorTok{());}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ selfSigned}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

For self-signed certificates, the chain validation in Step 4 will fail
unless you explicitly add the self-signed certificate as a trust anchor.
Most applications handle self-signed signatures by reporting: "The
signature is cryptographically valid, but the identity of the signer
could not be verified because the certificate is self-signed."

This is the correct behavior. A self-signed signature proves that
someone \emph{possessing that private key} signed the document. It does
not prove \emph{who} possesses that key, because no trusted third party
(CA) has vouched for the identity.

\subsection{Expired Certificates}\label{_expired_certificates_2}

Certificate expiration is time-dependent --- the same signature can be
"valid" today and "expired" tomorrow. The handling depends on whether a
timestamp is present:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Determine if a signature}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate is expired}\CommentTok{,}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ accounting for timestamps}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ CertExpirationStatus }\FunctionTok{checkCertExpiration}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{        X509CertificateHolder signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ timestampTime}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ now }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{Date}\OperatorTok{();}
    \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ notBefore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNotBefore}\OperatorTok{();}
    \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ notAfter }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getNotAfter}\OperatorTok{();}

    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{now}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{before}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{notAfter}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ CertExpirationStatus}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{VALID}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Certificate has expired — check if we have a timestamp}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{timestampTime }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{timestampTime}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{before}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{notAfter}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{\&\&}\NormalTok{ timestampTime}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{after}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{notBefore}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate expired on \{\}, but timestamp \{\} "} \OperatorTok{+}
                    \StringTok{"proves signature was created during validity period"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    notAfter}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ timestampTime}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ CertExpirationStatus}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{EXPIRED\_BUT\_TIMESTAMPED}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{else} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate expired on \{\}, and timestamp \{\} "} \OperatorTok{+}
                    \StringTok{"is outside the validity period [\{\} to \{\}]"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                    notAfter}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ timestampTime}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ notBefore}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ notAfter}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ CertExpirationStatus}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{EXPIRED\_AND\_TIMESTAMP\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Certificate expired on \{\} and no timestamp is present"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            notAfter}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ CertExpirationStatus}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{EXPIRED\_NO\_TIMESTAMP}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Missing Intermediate
Certificates}\label{_missing_intermediate_certificates}

The most common real-world chain validation failure is a missing
intermediate certificate. The CMS \texttt{SignedData} should include all
intermediate certificates between the signer's certificate and the root,
but some signing implementations omit them.

When intermediates are missing, you have two options:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{AIA chasing}: Follow the Authority Information Access (AIA)
  extension in the signer's certificate to download the missing
  intermediate from the CA's server
\item
  \textbf{Local certificate cache}: Maintain a local cache of known
  intermediate certificates
\end{enumerate}

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Attempt to fetch missing intermediate certificates via AIA}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{cert}\CommentTok{  }\NormalTok{the certificate whose issuer we need}
 \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@return}\NormalTok{ the issuer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s certificate}\CommentTok{,}\NormalTok{ or null if not fetchable}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate} \FunctionTok{fetchIssuerViaAia}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ aiaExtBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getExtensionValue}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            Extension}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{authorityInfoAccess}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getId}\OperatorTok{());}
    \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{aiaExtBytes }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"No AIA extension — cannot fetch issuer"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    ASN1OctetString aiaOctet }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ ASN1OctetString}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{aiaExtBytes}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{    AuthorityInformationAccess aia }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{            AuthorityInformationAccess}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    ASN1Sequence}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{aiaOctet}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getOctets}\OperatorTok{()));}

    \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{AccessDescription desc }\OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ aia}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAccessDescriptions}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{desc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAccessMethod}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{equals}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                AccessDescription}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_ad\_caIssuers}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            GeneralName gn }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ desc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAccessLocation}\OperatorTok{();}
            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{gn}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTagNo}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ GeneralName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{uniformResourceIdentifier}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
                \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ url }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ gn}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getName}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{toString}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{                log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Fetching issuer certificate from AIA: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ url}\OperatorTok{);}

                \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{InputStream}\NormalTok{ is }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{URL}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{url}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{openStream}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
                    \BuiltInTok{CertificateFactory}\NormalTok{ cf }\OperatorTok{=}
                            \BuiltInTok{CertificateFactory}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"X.509"}\OperatorTok{);}
                    \ControlFlowTok{return} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ cf}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{generateCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{is}\OperatorTok{);}
                \OperatorTok{\}}
            \OperatorTok{\}}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{    log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{warn}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"AIA extension present but no CA Issuers URL found"}\OperatorTok{);}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

In production, always cache downloaded certificates. AIA chasing adds
network latency and introduces a dependency on external servers ---
exactly the kind of dependency that LTV (Chapter 18) is designed to
eliminate. If you are building a signing service, embed all intermediate
certificates in the CMS at signing time, so validators never need to
chase AIA.

\section{Algorithm OID Mapping
Utility}\label{_algorithm_oid_mapping_utility}

Throughout this chapter, we reference a \texttt{mapOidToAlgorithm()}
helper. Here is a complete implementation that covers the algorithm OIDs
you will encounter in real PDF signatures:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Map a digest or signature algorithm OID to a Java algorithm name}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Covers the algorithms used in real}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{world PDF signatures}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{mapOidToAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ oid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
    \ControlFlowTok{return} \ControlFlowTok{switch} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{oid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// Digest algorithms}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.3.14.3.2.26"}         \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}1"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.1"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}256"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.2"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}384"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.3"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}512"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \CommentTok{// RSA signature algorithms}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.5"}  \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA1withRSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.11"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA256withRSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.12"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA384withRSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.13"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA512withRSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.113549.1.1.10"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"RSASSA{-}PSS"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \CommentTok{// ECDSA signature algorithms}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.10045.4.3.2"}   \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA256withECDSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.10045.4.3.3"}   \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA384withECDSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.2.840.10045.4.3.4"}   \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA512withECDSA"}\OperatorTok{;}
        \KeywordTok{default} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \ControlFlowTok{throw} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{IllegalArgumentException}\OperatorTok{(}
                \StringTok{"Unknown algorithm OID: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ oid}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\};}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Key Takeaways}\label{_key_takeaways_16}

\begin{itemize}
\item
  PDFBox provides signature extraction via two paths: the AcroForm field
  tree (full metadata) or \texttt{getSignatureDictionaries()} (fast,
  crypto-only)
\item
  CMS parsing requires both the \texttt{/Contents} bytes \emph{and} the
  signed content (byte range data) --- always pass both to
  \texttt{CMSSignedData}
\item
  The five-step pipeline is: byte range check, hash verification, CMS
  cryptographic verification, certificate chain validation, trust
  evaluation
\item
  \texttt{SignerInformation.verify()} performs Steps 2 and 3 combined
  --- but not chain validation or trust evaluation
\item
  Java's PKIX \texttt{CertPathBuilder} handles chain construction,
  validity checking, and basic constraints automatically
\item
  Timestamps shift the validation time from "now" to "when the signature
  was created" --- critical for expired certificates
\item
  Revision comparison detects modifications between signatures but is
  not a complete security guarantee
\item
  Self-signed signatures are cryptographically valid but untrusted ---
  always report the distinction clearly
\item
  Missing intermediates are the most common chain failure --- implement
  AIA chasing or embed all intermediates at signing time
\item
  The \texttt{VerificationReport} model gives callers granular per-step
  results rather than a binary pass/fail
\end{itemize}

\chapter{Test Cases \& Reference PDFs}\label{_test_cases_reference_pdfs}

Testing PDF digital signatures is one of the hardest testing problems in
software engineering. A signature that is valid today may be invalid
tomorrow because a certificate expired. A test that passes on your
machine may fail on CI because the TSA server is unreachable. A
verification test that ran perfectly for three years suddenly breaks
because a CRL distribution point returns HTTP 404. Time, network, and
third-party infrastructure are all embedded in your test surface --- and
they are all moving targets.

This chapter covers how to build a test infrastructure that is
deterministic, fast, and comprehensive. We generate our own keystores,
mock external services, freeze time, and construct a test matrix that
exercises every meaningful signature scenario. By the end, you will have
a test suite that runs in seconds, needs no network access, and covers
the full signing-to-verification round trip including PAdES compliance.

\section{Why Testing PDF Signatures Is
Hard}\label{_why_testing_pdf_signatures_is_hard}

\subsection{The Three Enemies of Deterministic Signature
Tests}\label{_the_three_enemies_of_deterministic_signature_tests}

\textbf{Enemy 1: Time.} Certificate validation is time-dependent. A
certificate that is valid right now has a \texttt{notBefore} and
\texttt{notAfter} date range. If your test generates a certificate valid
for one year and your test suite runs 13 months later, every test that
validates that certificate will fail. Timestamps make this worse --- a
timestamp proves the signature was created at a specific time, and the
TSA certificate has its own validity period.

\textbf{Enemy 2: Network.} Real signing workflows depend on external
services: TSA servers for timestamps, OCSP responders for revocation
checks, CRL distribution points for certificate revocation lists. Any of
these can be slow, unreachable, or returning unexpected responses. A
test that calls a real TSA is a test that depends on the internet ---
and the internet is not deterministic.

\textbf{Enemy 3: State.} PDF files are stateful. A signed PDF is a
specific sequence of bytes, and any change --- even adding a single
trailing newline --- invalidates the signature. Tests that generate PDFs
must produce identical byte sequences across runs, or the test
assertions become meaningless. Different versions of PDFBox may
serialize objects in different orders, produce different padding sizes,
or use different default values --- any of which changes the output
bytes.

\subsection{The Testing Strategy}\label{_the_testing_strategy}

Our approach addresses all three enemies:

\begin{enumerate}
\def\labelenumi{\arabic{enumi}.}
\item
  \textbf{Generate test keystores programmatically} with explicit,
  long-lived validity periods (100 years --- we are not testing
  certificate expiration unless we want to)
\item
  \textbf{Mock all external services} (TSA, OCSP) using WireMock or
  BouncyCastle's in-process TSA
\item
  \textbf{Freeze time} using a \texttt{Clock} abstraction so certificate
  and timestamp validation happens at a controlled instant
\item
  \textbf{Generate reference PDFs as part of the test setup} rather than
  shipping binary PDF files in the repository
\end{enumerate}

\section{Test Infrastructure: JUnit 5 and Test Keystore
Generation}\label{_test_infrastructure_junit_5_and_test_keystore_generation}

\subsection{The Test Keystore
Generator}\label{_the_test_keystore_generator}

Every test needs cryptographic key material: a signing certificate, an
intermediate CA, a root CA, and a TSA certificate. We generate all of
these programmatically using BouncyCastle:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Generates a complete PKI hierarchy for testing}\CommentTok{:}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Root CA }\CommentTok{{-}\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ Intermediate CA }\CommentTok{{-}\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ Signer certificate}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Plus a separate TSA certificate signed by the Root CA}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\AttributeTok{@Slf4j}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ TestKeystoreGenerator }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{final} \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ KEY\_SIZE }\OperatorTok{=} \DecValTok{2048}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{final} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ SIGNATURE\_ALGORITHM }\OperatorTok{=} \StringTok{"SHA256withRSA"}\OperatorTok{;}

    \CommentTok{/**}
     \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Generate a complete test PKI and return it as an in}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{memory KeyStore}\CommentTok{.}
     \CommentTok{*/}
    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore} \FunctionTok{generateTestKeystore}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{char}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{)}
            \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \CommentTok{// Root CA — self{-}signed, 100{-}year validity}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyPair}\NormalTok{ rootKeyPair }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ rootCert }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateRootCaCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                rootKeyPair}\OperatorTok{,}
                \StringTok{"CN=Test Root CA, O=PDFalyzer Test, C=DE"}\OperatorTok{,}
                \DecValTok{100}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Intermediate CA — signed by Root, 50{-}year validity}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyPair}\NormalTok{ intermediateKeyPair }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ intermediateCert }\OperatorTok{=}
                \FunctionTok{generateIntermediateCaCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        intermediateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{,}
                        \StringTok{"CN=Test Intermediate CA, O=PDFalyzer Test, C=DE"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                        rootKeyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                        rootCert}\OperatorTok{,}
                        \DecValTok{50}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Signer — signed by Intermediate, 10{-}year validity}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyPair}\NormalTok{ signerKeyPair }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ signerCert }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateSignerCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                signerKeyPair}\OperatorTok{,}
                \StringTok{"CN=Test Signer, O=PDFalyzer Test, C=DE"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                intermediateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                intermediateCert}\OperatorTok{,}
                \DecValTok{10}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// TSA — signed by Root, 50{-}year validity, with timeStamping EKU}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyPair}\NormalTok{ tsaKeyPair }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ tsaCert }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateTsaCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                tsaKeyPair}\OperatorTok{,}
                \StringTok{"CN=Test TSA, O=PDFalyzer Test, C=DE"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                rootKeyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                rootCert}\OperatorTok{,}
                \DecValTok{50}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Assemble into a PKCS12 KeyStore}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\NormalTok{ ks }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"PKCS12"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Store the signer\textquotesingle{}s key + full chain}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signerChain }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ intermediateCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootCert}
        \OperatorTok{\};}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setKeyEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"signer"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerKeyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                password}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerChain}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Store the TSA\textquotesingle{}s key + chain}
        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ tsaChain }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{\{}\NormalTok{ tsaCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootCert }\OperatorTok{\};}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setKeyEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"tsa"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsaKeyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                password}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsaChain}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Store the root as a trusted cert entry}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCertificateEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"root"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootCert}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Generated test keystore with signer, TSA, and root"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ks}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{KeyPair} \FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{()} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyPairGenerator}\NormalTok{ gen }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{KeyPairGenerator}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"RSA"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        gen}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{initialize}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{KEY\_SIZE}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ gen}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{generateKeyPair}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate} \FunctionTok{generateRootCaCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}
            \BuiltInTok{KeyPair}\NormalTok{ keyPair}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ dn}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ validityYears}\OperatorTok{)}
            \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{        X500Name issuer }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{X500Name}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{dn}\OperatorTok{);}
        \BuiltInTok{BigInteger}\NormalTok{ serial }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{BigInteger}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{valueOf}\OperatorTok{(}
                \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{currentTimeMillis}\OperatorTok{());}
        \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ notBefore }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{Date}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ notAfter }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Date}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{from}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Instant}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{now}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{plus}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Duration}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{ofDays}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{365L} \OperatorTok{*}\NormalTok{ validityYears}\OperatorTok{)));}

\NormalTok{        JcaX509v3CertificateBuilder builder }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaX509v3CertificateBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        issuer}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ serial}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ notBefore}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ notAfter}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                        issuer}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ keyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPublic}\OperatorTok{());}

        \CommentTok{// CA extensions}
\NormalTok{        builder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addExtension}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Extension}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{basicConstraints}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{,}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{BasicConstraints}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{        builder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addExtension}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Extension}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{keyUsage}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{,}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{KeyUsage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{KeyUsage}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{keyCertSign} \OperatorTok{|}\NormalTok{ KeyUsage}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{cRLSign}\OperatorTok{));}

\NormalTok{        ContentSigner signer }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaContentSignerBuilder}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                SIGNATURE\_ALGORITHM}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{keyPair}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{());}

        \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaX509CertificateConverter}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{builder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signer}\OperatorTok{));}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{// Similar methods for intermediate, signer, and TSA certificates}
    \CommentTok{// omitted for brevity — full listings in Appendix A}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{JUnit 5 Test Base Class}\label{_junit_5_test_base_class}

We use a shared base class that sets up the test PKI and provides
utility methods:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\AttributeTok{@Slf4j}
\KeywordTok{abstract} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ SignatureTestBase }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\NormalTok{ testKeystore}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ signerKey}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signerChain}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\NormalTok{ tsaKey}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ tsaChain}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{;}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{final} \DataTypeTok{char}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ PASSWORD }\OperatorTok{=} \StringTok{"test"}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toCharArray}\OperatorTok{();}

    \AttributeTok{@BeforeAll}
    \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{setupPki}\OperatorTok{()} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \BuiltInTok{Security}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{BouncyCastleProvider}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{        testKeystore }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ TestKeystoreGenerator}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{generateTestKeystore}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PASSWORD}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        signerKey }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ testKeystore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getKey}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"signer"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ PASSWORD}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        signerChain }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Arrays}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{stream}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                testKeystore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificateChain}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"signer"}\OperatorTok{))}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{map}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{c }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ c}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toArray}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]::}\KeywordTok{new}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        tsaKey }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{PrivateKey}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ testKeystore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getKey}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"tsa"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ PASSWORD}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        tsaChain }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Arrays}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{stream}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                testKeystore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificateChain}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"tsa"}\OperatorTok{))}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{map}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{c }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ c}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toArray}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]::}\KeywordTok{new}\OperatorTok{);}

        \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ rootCert }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{)}
\NormalTok{                testKeystore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"root"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        trustAnchors }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{of}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{rootCert}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{));}

\NormalTok{        log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{info}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Test PKI initialized — signer: \{\}, root: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                signerChain}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{].}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{(),}
\NormalTok{                rootCert}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSubjectX500Principal}\OperatorTok{());}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{/**}
     \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Generate a minimal unsigned PDF for testing}\CommentTok{.}
     \CommentTok{*/}
    \KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{generateTestPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDDocument}\OperatorTok{())} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            PDPage page }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDPage}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addPage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{page}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDPageContentStream cs }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDPageContentStream}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ page}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{beginText}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFont}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA}\OperatorTok{),} \DecValTok{12}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{newLineAtOffset}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{72}\OperatorTok{,} \DecValTok{700}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{showText}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{content}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{endText}\OperatorTok{();}
            \OperatorTok{\}}
            \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ baos }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{save}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{baos}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ baos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toByteArray}\OperatorTok{();}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Test PDF Generation
Scenarios}\label{_test_pdf_generation_scenarios}

\subsection{Generating Signed PDFs
Programmatically}\label{_generating_signed_pdfs_programmatically}

Rather than shipping pre-built signed PDFs (which become stale and
opaque), we generate them as part of each test. This ensures every test
controls its own input and can vary specific parameters:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Sign a PDF with the test keystore and return the signed bytes}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{signPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,} \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ addTimestamp}\OperatorTok{,}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ subFilter}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDDocument doc }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Loader}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{loadPDF}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        PDSignature pdSig }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        pdSig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FILTER\_ADOBE\_PPKLITE}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        pdSig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSubFilter}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{subFilter}\OperatorTok{));}
\NormalTok{        pdSig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Test Signer"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        pdSig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setReason}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Integration test"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        pdSig}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setSignDate}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{        SignatureInterface sigInterface }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{TestSignatureInterface}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                        signerKey}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerChain}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                        addTimestamp }\OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ tsaKey }\OperatorTok{:} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                        addTimestamp }\OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ tsaChain }\OperatorTok{:} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addSignature}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdSig}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ sigInterface}\OperatorTok{);}

        \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\NormalTok{ baos }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ByteArrayOutputStream}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{saveIncremental}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{baos}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ baos}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toByteArray}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\subsection{Scenario Generators}\label{_scenario_generators}

Each test scenario generates a specific type of signed PDF:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Generate a PDF signed with different configurations}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\KeywordTok{protected} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{generateScenario}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{TestScenario scenario}\OperatorTok{)}
        \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}

    \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdf }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{generateTestPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Scenario: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ scenario}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{name}\OperatorTok{());}

    \ControlFlowTok{return} \ControlFlowTok{switch} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{scenario}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ BASIC\_PKCS7 }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"adbe.pkcs7.detached"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ BASIC\_PADES }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ PADES\_WITH\_TIMESTAMP }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{true}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"ETSI.CAdES.detached"}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ SELF\_SIGNED }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdfWithSelfSigned}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ EXPIRED\_CERT }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdfWithExpiredCert}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ MULTI\_SIGNATURE }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdfTwice}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ TAMPERED\_AFTER\_SIGNING }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{tamperSignedPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\FunctionTok{signPdf}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \StringTok{"adbe.pkcs7.detached"}\OperatorTok{));}
        \ControlFlowTok{case}\NormalTok{ MISSING\_INTERMEDIATE }\OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}}
            \FunctionTok{signPdfWithMissingIntermediate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdf}\OperatorTok{);}
    \OperatorTok{\};}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Test Matrix: Scenario to Expected
Result}\label{_test_matrix_scenario_to_expected_result}

The following table defines every test scenario, what it tests, and the
expected outcome at each verification step. This is the specification
for your test suite --- every row should be a test method:

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}

  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 12\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2727}}@{}}
\caption{Signature test matrix}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright

\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright

\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Scenario
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
ByteRange
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Hash
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
CMS
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Chain
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
