\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{asn1}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{AttributeTable}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{asn1}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pkcs}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PKCSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cert}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaX509CertificateConverter}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{CMSProcessableByteArray}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignerInformation}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SignerInformationStore}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cms}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{jcajce}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{JcaSimpleSignerInfoVerifierBuilder}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{tsp}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{TimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{bouncycastle}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Store}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Complete five}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{step signature verification service}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Accepts an injectable Clock for deterministic testing}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\AttributeTok{@Slf4j}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ VerificationService }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{final}\NormalTok{ Clock clock}\OperatorTok{;}

    \KeywordTok{public} \FunctionTok{VerificationService}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{clock} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ Clock}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{systemUTC}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{public} \FunctionTok{VerificationService}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{Clock clock}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \KeywordTok{this}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{clock} \OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ clock}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \CommentTok{/**}
     \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Run the full five}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{step verification pipeline}\CommentTok{.}
     \CommentTok{*/}
    \KeywordTok{public}\NormalTok{ VerificationReport }\FunctionTok{verify}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            PDSignature signature}\OperatorTok{,}
            \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{        VerificationReport report }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{VerificationReport}\OperatorTok{();}

        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \CommentTok{// Step 1: Byte range}
            \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getByteRange}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setByteRangeResult}\OperatorTok{(}
                    \FunctionTok{verifyByteRange}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length}\OperatorTok{));}

            \CommentTok{// Step 2: Hash}
            \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signedContent }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{extractSignedContent}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            CMSSignedData cmsData }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSProcessableByteArray}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedContent}\OperatorTok{),}
\NormalTok{                    signature}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getContents}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{            SignerInformation signerInfo }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignerInfos}\OperatorTok{()}
                    \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSigners}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}

\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setHashResult}\OperatorTok{(}\FunctionTok{verifyHash}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    signedContent}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{));}

            \CommentTok{// Step 3: CMS}
\NormalTok{            X509CertificateHolder signerCert }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \FunctionTok{verifyCms}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCmsResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}
                    \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                    \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{CMS\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{);}

            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{;}

            \CommentTok{// Check for timestamp}
            \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ validationTime }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{Date}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{from}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{clock}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{instant}\OperatorTok{());}
            \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ tsTime }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{extractTimestampTime}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerInfo}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsTime }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                validationTime }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsTime}\OperatorTok{;}
\NormalTok{                report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTimestampTime}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsTime}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{                report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTimestampResult}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{);}
            \OperatorTok{\}}

            \CommentTok{// Step 4: Certificate chain}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setChainResult}\OperatorTok{(}\FunctionTok{validateChain}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ validationTime}\OperatorTok{));}

            \CommentTok{// Step 5: Trust}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setTrustResult}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                    report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getChainResult}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{==}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                            \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                            \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{NOT\_TRUSTED}\OperatorTok{);}

        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{catch} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Exception}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Verification failed"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            report}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setException}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{e}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ report}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]} \FunctionTok{extractSignedContent}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}
            \DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ content }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[}\NormalTok{byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]];}
        \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{arraycopy}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{,}
                \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{]);}
        \BuiltInTok{System}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{arraycopy}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{pdfBytes}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{                byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{1}\OperatorTok{],}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{]);}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ content}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult }\FunctionTok{verifyByteRange}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{int}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{,}
            \DataTypeTok{long}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{||}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{length} \OperatorTok{!=} \DecValTok{4}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_MALFORMED}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{!=} \DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_NOT\_AT\_START}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
        \DataTypeTok{long}\NormalTok{ end }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{long}\OperatorTok{)}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{2}\OperatorTok{]} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ byteRange}\OperatorTok{[}\DecValTok{3}\OperatorTok{];}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{end }\OperatorTok{!=}\NormalTok{ fileSize}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{BYTE\_RANGE\_TRAILING\_BYTES}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult }\FunctionTok{verifyHash}\OperatorTok{(}\DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ signedContent}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            SignerInformation signerInfo}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ algName }\OperatorTok{=} \FunctionTok{mapOidToAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDigestAlgOID}\OperatorTok{());}
        \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\NormalTok{ md }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{algName}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ computed }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ md}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{digest}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signedContent}\OperatorTok{);}

        \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\NormalTok{ digestAttr }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{CMSAttributes}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{messageDigest}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ claimed }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ ASN1OctetString}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                digestAttr}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAttrValues}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getObjectAt}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{)).}\FunctionTok{getOctets}\OperatorTok{();}

        \ControlFlowTok{return} \BuiltInTok{MessageDigest}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEqual}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{computed}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ claimed}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}
                \OperatorTok{:}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HASH\_MISMATCH}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \AttributeTok{@SuppressWarnings}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"unchecked"}\OperatorTok{)}
    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ X509CertificateHolder }\FunctionTok{verifyCms}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{CMSSignedData cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            SignerInformation signerInfo}\OperatorTok{)} \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        Store}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ certStore }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{();}
        \BuiltInTok{Collection}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ matches }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                certStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getSID}\OperatorTok{());}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{matches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{isEmpty}\OperatorTok{())} \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}

\NormalTok{        X509CertificateHolder cert }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ matches}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{iterator}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{next}\OperatorTok{();}
        \DataTypeTok{boolean}\NormalTok{ valid }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{verify}\OperatorTok{(}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaSimpleSignerInfoVerifierBuilder}\OperatorTok{()}
                        \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{cert}\OperatorTok{));}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ valid }\OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ cert }\OperatorTok{:} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private} \BuiltInTok{Date} \FunctionTok{extractTimestampTime}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{SignerInformation signerInfo}\OperatorTok{)}
            \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{Exception} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{        AttributeTable unsigned }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ signerInfo}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getUnsignedAttributes}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{unsigned }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}

        \BuiltInTok{Attribute}\NormalTok{ tsAttr }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ unsigned}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                PKCSObjectIdentifiers}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{id\_aa\_signatureTimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{);}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsAttr }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \ControlFlowTok{return} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{;}

        \DataTypeTok{byte}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ tsBytes }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ tsAttr}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getAttrValues}\OperatorTok{()}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getObjectAt}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{toASN1Primitive}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getEncoded}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        TimeStampToken tst }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{TimeStampToken}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{CMSSignedData}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{tsBytes}\OperatorTok{));}
        \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ tst}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTimeStampInfo}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getGenTime}\OperatorTok{();}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult }\FunctionTok{validateChain}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            X509CertificateHolder signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}
\NormalTok{            CMSSignedData cmsData}\OperatorTok{,}
            \BuiltInTok{Set}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{TrustAnchor}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}
            \BuiltInTok{Date}\NormalTok{ validationTime}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{try} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            JcaX509CertificateConverter converter }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{JcaX509CertificateConverter}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{setProvider}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{);}
            \BuiltInTok{List}\OperatorTok{\textless{}}\BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{\textgreater{}}\NormalTok{ allCerts }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{ArrayList}\OperatorTok{\textless{}\textgreater{}();}
            \ControlFlowTok{for} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{X509CertificateHolder h }\OperatorTok{:}
\NormalTok{                    cmsData}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificates}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getMatches}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{))} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{                allCerts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{converter}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{h}\OperatorTok{));}
            \OperatorTok{\}}

            \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\NormalTok{ target }\OperatorTok{=}
\NormalTok{                    converter}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signerCert}\OperatorTok{);}
            \BuiltInTok{X509CertSelector}\NormalTok{ selector }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{X509CertSelector}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{            selector}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCertificate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{target}\OperatorTok{);}

            \BuiltInTok{PKIXBuilderParameters}\NormalTok{ params }\OperatorTok{=}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{PKIXBuilderParameters}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{trustAnchors}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ selector}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{addCertStore}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{CertStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Collection"}\OperatorTok{,}
                    \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{CollectionCertStoreParameters}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{allCerts}\OperatorTok{)));}
\NormalTok{            params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setRevocationEnabled}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            params}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setDate}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{validationTime}\OperatorTok{);}

            \BuiltInTok{CertPathBuilder}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"PKIX"}\OperatorTok{,} \StringTok{"BC"}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{build}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{params}\OperatorTok{);}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{OK}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}} \ControlFlowTok{catch} \OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{Exception}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            log}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{error}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"Chain validation failed: \{\}"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ e}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getMessage}\OperatorTok{());}
            \ControlFlowTok{return}\NormalTok{ ValidationResult}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{CHAIN\_INVALID}\OperatorTok{;}
        \OperatorTok{\}}
    \OperatorTok{\}}

    \KeywordTok{private} \DataTypeTok{static} \BuiltInTok{String} \FunctionTok{mapOidToAlgorithm}\OperatorTok{(}\BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ oid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
        \ControlFlowTok{return} \ControlFlowTok{switch} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{oid}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
            \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"1.3.14.3.2.26"}          \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}1"}\OperatorTok{;}
            \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.1"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}256"}\OperatorTok{;}
            \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.2"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}384"}\OperatorTok{;}
            \ControlFlowTok{case} \StringTok{"2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.3"} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \StringTok{"SHA{-}512"}\OperatorTok{;}
            \KeywordTok{default} \OperatorTok{{-}\textgreater{}} \ControlFlowTok{throw} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{IllegalArgumentException}\OperatorTok{(}
                    \StringTok{"Unknown digest OID: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ oid}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\};}
    \OperatorTok{\}}
\OperatorTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Signature Appearance
Builder}\label{_signature_appearance_builder}

A minimal but complete appearance builder for visible signatures.
Generates an appearance stream with signer name, date, and reason inside
the signature widget rectangle.

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\KeywordTok{package}\ImportTok{ io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfalyzer}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{signing}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{io}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{IOException}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{text}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{SimpleDateFormat}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{util}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Calendar}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{lombok}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{extern}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{slf4j}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Slf4j}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{cos}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDDocument}\OperatorTok{;}

\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{font}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{annotation}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDAnnotationWidget}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{digitalsignature}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDSignature}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDAcroForm}\OperatorTok{;}
\KeywordTok{import} \ImportTok{org}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{interactive}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\ImportTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{;}

\CommentTok{/**}
 \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Builds a visible signature appearance with text content}\CommentTok{.}
 \CommentTok{*/}
\AttributeTok{@Slf4j}
\KeywordTok{public} \KeywordTok{class}\NormalTok{ AppearanceBuilder }\OperatorTok{\{}

    \CommentTok{/**}
     \CommentTok{*}\NormalTok{ Create a visible signature field with a text}\CommentTok{{-}}\NormalTok{based appearance}\CommentTok{.}
     \CommentTok{*}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{doc}\CommentTok{       }\NormalTok{the document to add the appearance to}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{sig}\CommentTok{       }\NormalTok{the PDSignature to associate with the field}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{page}\CommentTok{      }\NormalTok{the page number }\CommentTok{(0{-}}\NormalTok{based}\CommentTok{)}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{rect}\CommentTok{      }\NormalTok{the rectangle for the signature widget}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{name}\CommentTok{      }\NormalTok{signer}\CommentTok{\textquotesingle{}}\NormalTok{s name}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{reason}\CommentTok{    }\NormalTok{signing reason}
     \CommentTok{*} \AnnotationTok{@param}\CommentTok{ }\AnnotationTok{signDate}\CommentTok{  }\NormalTok{the signing date}
     \CommentTok{*/}
    \KeywordTok{public} \DataTypeTok{static} \DataTypeTok{void} \FunctionTok{createVisibleAppearance}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{            PDDocument doc}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ PDSignature sig}\OperatorTok{,}
            \DataTypeTok{int}\NormalTok{ page}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ PDRectangle rect}\OperatorTok{,}
            \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ name}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ reason}\OperatorTok{,} \BuiltInTok{Calendar}\NormalTok{ signDate}\OperatorTok{)}
            \KeywordTok{throws} \BuiltInTok{IOException} \OperatorTok{\{}

\NormalTok{        PDAcroForm acroForm }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getAcroForm}\OperatorTok{();}
        \ControlFlowTok{if} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{acroForm }\OperatorTok{==} \KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{)} \OperatorTok{\{}
\NormalTok{            acroForm }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDAcroForm}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{            doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getDocumentCatalog}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{setAcroForm}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{acroForm}\OperatorTok{);}
        \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{        PDSignatureField sigField }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDSignatureField}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{acroForm}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        sigField}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setValue}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sig}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        acroForm}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getFields}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{add}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{sigField}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{        PDAnnotationWidget widget }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ sigField}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getWidgets}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{get}\OperatorTok{(}\DecValTok{0}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        widget}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setRectangle}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{rect}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        widget}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setPage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPage}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{page}\OperatorTok{));}

        \CommentTok{// Build appearance content}
\NormalTok{        PDType1Font font }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDType1Font}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                Standard14Fonts}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{FontName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{HELVETICA}\OperatorTok{);}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ fontSize }\OperatorTok{=} \FloatTok{8f}\OperatorTok{;}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ leading }\OperatorTok{=}\NormalTok{ fontSize }\OperatorTok{*} \FloatTok{1.4f}\OperatorTok{;}
        \DataTypeTok{float}\NormalTok{ margin }\OperatorTok{=} \FloatTok{4f}\OperatorTok{;}

        \BuiltInTok{String}\NormalTok{ dateStr }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{SimpleDateFormat}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"yyyy{-}MM{-}dd HH:mm:ss z"}\OperatorTok{)}
                \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{format}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{signDate}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getTime}\OperatorTok{());}

        \BuiltInTok{String}\OperatorTok{[]}\NormalTok{ lines }\OperatorTok{=} \OperatorTok{\{}
                \StringTok{"Digitally signed by: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ name}\OperatorTok{,}
                \StringTok{"Date: "} \OperatorTok{+}\NormalTok{ dateStr}\OperatorTok{,}
                \StringTok{"Reason: "} \OperatorTok{+} \OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{reason }\OperatorTok{!=} \KeywordTok{null} \OperatorTok{?}\NormalTok{ reason }\OperatorTok{:} \StringTok{"—"}\OperatorTok{)}
        \OperatorTok{\};}

        \CommentTok{// Create the appearance stream using form XObject}
\NormalTok{        PDRectangle formRect }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDRectangle}\OperatorTok{(}
\NormalTok{                rect}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getWidth}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ rect}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getHeight}\OperatorTok{());}

\NormalTok{        org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{graphics}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{PDFormXObject}\NormalTok{ form }\OperatorTok{=}
                \KeywordTok{new}\NormalTok{ org}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{apache}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdfbox}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{pdmodel}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{graphics}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{PDFormXObject}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{doc}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setBBox}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{formRect}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setResources}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{PDResources}\OperatorTok{());}
\NormalTok{        form}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getResources}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{put}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{COSName}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPDFName}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"F1"}\OperatorTok{),}\NormalTok{ font}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Write content stream operators directly}
        \BuiltInTok{StringBuilder}\NormalTok{ cs }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{StringBuilder}\OperatorTok{();}
\NormalTok{        cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"BT}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"/F1 "}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{fontSize}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Tf}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{margin}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" "}\OperatorTok{)}
          \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{rect}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getHeight}\OperatorTok{()} \OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ margin }\OperatorTok{{-}}\NormalTok{ fontSize}\OperatorTok{)}
          \OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" Td}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{leading}\OperatorTok{).}\FunctionTok{append}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{" TL}\SpecialCharTok{\textbackslash{}n}\StringTok{"}\OperatorTok{);}

        \OperatorTok{\}}

\NormalTok{                cs}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{toString}\OperatorTok{().}\FunctionTok{getBytes}\OperatorTok{(}\NormalTok{java}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{nio}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{charset}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{StandardCharsets}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{US\_ASCII}\OperatorTok{));}

        \CommentTok{// Intermediate CA}

\NormalTok{        X500Name intDn }\OperatorTok{=} \KeywordTok{new} \FunctionTok{X500Name}\OperatorTok{(}
                \StringTok{"CN=Test Intermediate CA, O=PDFalyzer Test, C=DE"}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{                intKp}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ intDn}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootKp}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootDn}\OperatorTok{,} \DecValTok{50}\OperatorTok{,} \KeywordTok{false}\OperatorTok{);}

\NormalTok{                tsaKp}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsaDn}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootKp}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootDn}\OperatorTok{,} \DecValTok{50}\OperatorTok{);}

        \CommentTok{// Assemble keystore}
        \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\NormalTok{ ks }\OperatorTok{=} \BuiltInTok{KeyStore}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getInstance}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"PKCS12"}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{load}\OperatorTok{(}\KeywordTok{null}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{);}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setKeyEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"signer"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ signerKp}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{,}
                \KeywordTok{new} \BuiltInTok{X509Certificate}\OperatorTok{[]\{}\NormalTok{ signerCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ intCert}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootCert }\OperatorTok{\});}
\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setKeyEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"tsa"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ tsaKp}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{getPrivate}\OperatorTok{(),}\NormalTok{ password}\OperatorTok{,}

\NormalTok{        ks}\OperatorTok{.}\FunctionTok{setCertificateEntry}\OperatorTok{(}\StringTok{"root"}\OperatorTok{,}\NormalTok{ rootCert}\OperatorTok{);}

\section{ASN.1 Structure Reference}\label{_asn_1_structure_reference}

This appendix is a reference card for the ASN.1 structures you will
encounter when working with PDF digital signatures. Every CMS
\texttt{SignedData} blob inside a \texttt{/Contents} value, every X.509
certificate in the chain, every OCSP response and timestamp token ---
they are all ASN.1 structures encoded in DER. When your BouncyCastle
code throws a \texttt{ParseException} at 2 AM, this is the appendix you
reach for.

We present the OID table first (because OIDs are the keys to
everything), then the ASN.1 grammar for each major structure, with
annotations showing where each field appears in the context of PDF
signatures.

\section{OID Reference Table}\label{_oid_reference_table}

Object Identifiers (OIDs) are the universal labels of the ASN.1 world.
Every algorithm, every attribute, every extension is identified by an
OID. The following table covers the OIDs you will encounter in PDF
signature processing:

\subsection{Digest Algorithms}\label{_digest_algorithms}

\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3750}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.3750}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 4\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}@{}}
\caption{Digest algorithm OIDs}\tabularnewline
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
OID
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Algorithm
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Status
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endfirsthead
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
OID
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Algorithm
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Status
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
1.3.14.3.2.26 & SHA-1 & Deprecated --- do not use for new signatures \\
2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.1 & SHA-256 & Recommended minimum \\
2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.2 & SHA-384 & Recommended for high security \\
2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.3 & SHA-512 & Recommended for high security \\
2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.4 & SHA-224 & Acceptable but uncommon \\
\end{longtable}

\section{CMS SignedData Structure (RFC 5652 --- Cryptographic Message
Syntax
(CMS))}\label{_cms_signeddata_structure_rfc_5652_cryptographic_message_syntax_cms}

The CMS \texttt{SignedData} is the core structure inside every PDF
\texttt{/Contents} value. This is the complete ASN.1 grammar from RFC
5652 --- Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS) Section 5.1, annotated with
PDF-specific notes:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{{-}{-} RFC 5652 Section 5.1}
\NormalTok{ContentInfo ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    contentType     ContentType,        }\CommentTok{{-}{-} OID 1.2.840.113549.1.7.2 (signedData)}
\NormalTok{    content     [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT ANY        }\CommentTok{{-}{-} The SignedData below}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{SignedData ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version             CMSVersion,         }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 1 for most PDF signatures}
\NormalTok{    digestAlgorithms    DigestAlgorithmIdentifiers,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} SET OF: usually \{sha{-}256\}}
\NormalTok{    encapContentInfo    EncapsulatedContentInfo,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} EMPTY for detached signatures (PDF)}
\NormalTok{    certificates    [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT CertificateSet }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} Signer cert + intermediates}
\NormalTok{    crls            [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT RevocationInfoChoices }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} Rarely used in PDF CMS}
\NormalTok{    signerInfos         SignerInfos         }\CommentTok{{-}{-} SET OF: usually exactly one}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} In PDF signatures, encapContentInfo.eContent is ABSENT because the}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} signed data (the byte range) is external to the CMS structure.}
\NormalTok{EncapsulatedContentInfo ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    eContentType    ContentType,        }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 1.2.840.113549.1.7.1 (id{-}data)}
\NormalTok{    eContent    [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{OCTET} \DataTypeTok{STRING} \KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
                                        \CommentTok{{-}{-} ABSENT for detached signatures}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{SignerInfo ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version                 CMSVersion,     }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 1 (issuerAndSerialNumber)}
\NormalTok{    sid                     SignerIdentifier,}
\NormalTok{    digestAlgorithm         DigestAlgorithmIdentifier,}
\NormalTok{    signedAttrs         [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT SignedAttributes }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} MUST be present for PDF sigs}
\NormalTok{    signatureAlgorithm      SignatureAlgorithmIdentifier,}
\NormalTok{    signature               SignatureValue,}
\NormalTok{    unsignedAttrs       [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT UnsignedAttributes }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} Timestamp lives here}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{SignerIdentifier ::= }\DataTypeTok{CHOICE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    issuerAndSerialNumber   IssuerAndSerialNumber,}
                                            \CommentTok{{-}{-} Most common in PDF}
\NormalTok{    subjectKeyIdentifier [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] SubjectKeyIdentifier}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{IssuerAndSerialNumber ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    issuer          Name,}
\NormalTok{    serialNumber    CertificateSerialNumber}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} Signed attributes (DER{-}encoded, then signed)}
\NormalTok{SignedAttributes ::= }\DataTypeTok{SET} \KeywordTok{SIZE}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{..MAX) }\DataTypeTok{OF}\NormalTok{ Attribute}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} The Attribute structure used for both signed and unsigned attrs}
\NormalTok{Attribute ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    attrType    OBJECT IDENTIFIER,      }\CommentTok{{-}{-} The attribute OID}
\NormalTok{    attrValues  }\DataTypeTok{SET} \DataTypeTok{OF}\NormalTok{ AttributeValue   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Usually exactly one value}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

For PDF detached signatures, the \texttt{eContent} field inside
\texttt{EncapsulatedContentInfo} is absent. The signed content is the
byte range data from the PDF file, which is passed externally to the CMS
verifier. This is why you must provide the byte range bytes when
constructing a \texttt{CMSSignedData} in BouncyCastle --- without them,
the verifier cannot compute the digest.

\section{X.509 v3 Certificate Structure (RFC 5280 --- Internet X.509 PKI
Certificate and CRL
Profile)}\label{_x_509_v3_certificate_structure_rfc_5280_internet_x_509_pki_certificate_and_crl_profile}

Every certificate in the CMS \texttt{certificates} collection, every CA
in the chain, and every TSA certificate follows this structure:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{{-}{-} RFC 5280 Section 4.1}
\NormalTok{Certificate ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    tbsCertificate      TBSCertificate,     }\CommentTok{{-}{-} The signed payload}
\NormalTok{    signatureAlgorithm  AlgorithmIdentifier, }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Must match inner sig alg}
\NormalTok{    signatureValue      BIT }\DataTypeTok{STRING}           \CommentTok{{-}{-} Issuer\textquotesingle{}s signature}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{TBSCertificate ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version         [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{DEFAULT}\NormalTok{ v1,    }\CommentTok{{-}{-} v3 (2)}
\NormalTok{    serialNumber        CertificateSerialNumber,}
\NormalTok{    signature           AlgorithmIdentifier,    }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Inner copy of sig alg}
\NormalTok{    issuer              Name,                   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} DN of the issuing CA}
\NormalTok{    validity            Validity,}
\NormalTok{    subject             Name,                   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} DN of the certificate owner}
\NormalTok{    subjectPublicKeyInfo SubjectPublicKeyInfo,}
    \CommentTok{{-}{-} v3 extensions follow:}
\NormalTok{    extensions      [}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT Extensions }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{Validity ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    notBefore   Time,       }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Certificate valid from this date}
\NormalTok{    notAfter    Time        }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Certificate valid until this date}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} Key extensions for PDF signature certificates:}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} keyUsage (2.5.29.15):}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   digitalSignature (bit 0) — required for signing certificates}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   nonRepudiation (bit 1) — alternative to digitalSignature}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   keyCertSign (bit 5) — required for CA certificates}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} basicConstraints (2.5.29.19):}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   cA: BOOLEAN — TRUE for CA certs, FALSE (or absent) for end{-}entity}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   pathLenConstraint: INTEGER — max chain depth below this CA}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} extendedKeyUsage (2.5.29.37):}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   id{-}kp{-}timeStamping (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.8) — required for TSA certs}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} authorityInfoAccess (1.3.6.1.5.5.7.1.1):}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   id{-}ad{-}caIssuers — URL to fetch the issuer\textquotesingle{}s certificate}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}   id{-}ad{-}ocsp — URL of the OCSP responder}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{OCSP Response Structure (RFC 6960 --- Online Certificate Status
Protocol
(OCSP))}\label{_ocsp_response_structure_rfc_6960_online_certificate_status_protocol_ocsp}

OCSP responses are embedded in the DSS (Document Security Store) for
PAdES-B-LT and PAdES-B-LTA signatures. The structure you will find in
\texttt{/DSS\ /OCSPs}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{{-}{-} RFC 6960 Section 4.2}
\NormalTok{OCSPResponse ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    responseStatus      OCSPResponseStatus,     }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 0 = successful}
\NormalTok{    responseBytes   [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT ResponseBytes }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{ResponseBytes ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    responseType    OBJECT IDENTIFIER,  }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 1.3.6.1.5.5.7.48.1.1 (basicResponse)}
\NormalTok{    response        }\DataTypeTok{OCTET} \DataTypeTok{STRING}        \CommentTok{{-}{-} DER{-}encoded BasicOCSPResponse}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{BasicOCSPResponse ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    tbsResponseData     ResponseData,}
\NormalTok{    signatureAlgorithm  AlgorithmIdentifier,}
\NormalTok{    signature           BIT }\DataTypeTok{STRING}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    certs           [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE} \DataTypeTok{OF}\NormalTok{ Certificate }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{ResponseData ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version         [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{DEFAULT}\NormalTok{ v1,}
\NormalTok{    responderID         ResponderID,}
\NormalTok{    producedAt          }\DataTypeTok{GeneralizedTime}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    responses           }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE} \DataTypeTok{OF}\NormalTok{ SingleResponse,}
\NormalTok{    responseExtensions  [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT Extensions }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{SingleResponse ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    certID              CertID,}
\NormalTok{    certStatus          CertStatus,         }\CommentTok{{-}{-} good | revoked | unknown}
\NormalTok{    thisUpdate          }\DataTypeTok{GeneralizedTime}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    nextUpdate      [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{GeneralizedTime} \KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    singleExtensions [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] EXPLICIT Extensions }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{CertStatus ::= }\DataTypeTok{CHOICE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    good        [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT }\DataTypeTok{NULL}\NormalTok{,          }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Certificate is not revoked}
\NormalTok{    revoked     [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT RevokedInfo,   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Certificate has been revoked}
\NormalTok{    unknown     [}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT UnknownInfo    }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Responder has no information}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Timestamp Token Structure (RFC 3161 --- Internet X.509 PKI
Time-Stamp Protocol
(TSP))}\label{_timestamp_token_structure_rfc_3161_internet_x_509_pki_time_stamp_protocol_tsp}

The timestamp token (TST) is the structure inside the
\texttt{id-aa-signatureTimeStampToken} unsigned attribute. It is itself
a CMS \texttt{SignedData} containing a \texttt{TSTInfo} as the
encapsulated content:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\CommentTok{{-}{-} RFC 3161 Section 2.4.2}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} The TimeStampToken is a CMS SignedData where the}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} encapContentInfo contains a TSTInfo:}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} id{-}ct{-}TSTInfo OBJECT IDENTIFIER ::= \{ 1.2.840.113549.1.9.16.1.4 \}}

\NormalTok{TSTInfo ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version         }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER}\NormalTok{ \{ v1(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \},}
\NormalTok{    policy          TSAPolicyId,            }\CommentTok{{-}{-} OID of the TSA\textquotesingle{}s policy}
\NormalTok{    messageImprint  MessageImprint,         }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Hash of the timestamped data}
\NormalTok{    serialNumber    }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER}\NormalTok{,                }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Unique per TSA}
\NormalTok{    genTime         }\DataTypeTok{GeneralizedTime}\NormalTok{,        }\CommentTok{{-}{-} The timestamp time (UTC)}
\NormalTok{    accuracy        Accuracy }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,      }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Time precision}
\NormalTok{    ordering        }\DataTypeTok{BOOLEAN} \KeywordTok{DEFAULT} \KeywordTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    nonce           }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,       }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Echoed from request}
\NormalTok{    tsa         [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] GeneralName }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} TSA identity}
\NormalTok{    extensions  [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT Extensions }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{MessageImprint ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    hashAlgorithm   AlgorithmIdentifier,    }\CommentTok{{-}{-} e.g., SHA{-}256}
\NormalTok{    hashedMessage   }\DataTypeTok{OCTET} \DataTypeTok{STRING}            \CommentTok{{-}{-} Hash of the signature value}
\NormalTok{\}}

\NormalTok{Accuracy ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    seconds     }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    millis  [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{..}\DecValTok{999}\NormalTok{) }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    micros  [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER}\NormalTok{ (}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{..}\DecValTok{999}\NormalTok{) }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} The Timestamp Request (sent to the TSA):}
\NormalTok{TimeStampReq ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    version         }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER}\NormalTok{ \{ v1(}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{) \},}
\NormalTok{    messageImprint  MessageImprint,     }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Hash to timestamp}
\NormalTok{    reqPolicy       TSAPolicyId }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    nonce           }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}\NormalTok{,   }\CommentTok{{-}{-} Anti{-}replay}
\NormalTok{    certReq         }\DataTypeTok{BOOLEAN} \KeywordTok{DEFAULT} \KeywordTok{FALSE}\NormalTok{,}
                                        \CommentTok{{-}{-} Request TSA cert in response}
\NormalTok{    extensions  [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] IMPLICIT Extensions }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} The Timestamp Response (returned by the TSA):}
\NormalTok{TimeStampResp ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    status          PKIStatusInfo,      }\CommentTok{{-}{-} 0 = granted}
\NormalTok{    timeStampToken  TimeStampToken }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
                                        \CommentTok{{-}{-} CMS SignedData containing TSTInfo}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

The \texttt{messageImprint} in the TSTInfo is the hash of the
\emph{signature value} (the raw bytes of the
\texttt{SignerInfo.signature} field), not the hash of the signed
document. This is a critical distinction. The timestamp proves that a
specific signature value existed at a specific time. The signature
value, in turn, covers the document bytes (via the signed attributes and
byte range). The chain of trust is: timestamp proves signature time,
signature proves document integrity.

\section{Algorithm Identifier
Structure}\label{_algorithm_identifier_structure}

The \texttt{AlgorithmIdentifier} appears throughout all the structures
above. For most algorithms, the parameters field is either absent or
\texttt{NULL}:

\begin{Shaded}
\begin{Highlighting}[]
\NormalTok{AlgorithmIdentifier ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    algorithm   OBJECT IDENTIFIER,}
\NormalTok{    parameters  ANY DEFINED BY algorithm }\KeywordTok{OPTIONAL}
\NormalTok{\}}

\CommentTok{{-}{-} For SHA{-}256: algorithm = 2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.1, parameters = NULL (or absent)}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} For RSA:     algorithm = 1.2.840.113549.1.1.11, parameters = NULL}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} For ECDSA:   algorithm = 1.2.840.10045.4.3.2, parameters = absent}
\CommentTok{{-}{-} For RSA{-}PSS: algorithm = 1.2.840.113549.1.1.10,}
\CommentTok{{-}{-}              parameters = RSASSA{-}PSS{-}params (complex — see RFC 4055)}

\NormalTok{RSASSA{-}PSS{-}params ::= }\DataTypeTok{SEQUENCE}\NormalTok{ \{}
\NormalTok{    hashAlgorithm       [}\DecValTok{0}\NormalTok{] HashAlgorithm }\KeywordTok{DEFAULT}\NormalTok{ sha1,}
\NormalTok{    maskGenAlgorithm    [}\DecValTok{1}\NormalTok{] MaskGenAlgorithm }\KeywordTok{DEFAULT}\NormalTok{ mgf1SHA1,}
\NormalTok{    saltLength          [}\DecValTok{2}\NormalTok{] }\DataTypeTok{INTEGER} \KeywordTok{DEFAULT} \DecValTok{20}\NormalTok{,}
\NormalTok{    trailerField        [}\DecValTok{3}\NormalTok{] TrailerField }\KeywordTok{DEFAULT}\NormalTok{ trailerFieldBC}
\NormalTok{\}}
\end{Highlighting}
\end{Shaded}

\section{Glossary \& Spec References}\label{_glossary_spec_references}

This appendix collects every acronym, term, and specification referenced
throughout the book into two lookup tables. When you encounter an
unfamiliar term in a chapter, this is the place to find a concise
definition. When you need the precise specification for a standard we
reference, the spec table gives you the document number, title, and
where to find it.

\section{Glossary}\label{_glossary}

{\def\LTcaptype{none} % do not increment counter
\begin{longtable}[]{@{}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 2\tabcolsep) * \real{0.2500}}
  >{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{(\linewidth - 2\tabcolsep) * \real{0.7500}}@{}}
\toprule\noalign{}
\begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Term
\end{minipage} & \begin{minipage}[b]{\linewidth}\raggedright
Definition
\end{minipage} \\
\midrule\noalign{}
\endhead
\bottomrule\noalign{}
\endlastfoot
AATL & \textbf{Adobe Approved Trust List.} Adobe's curated list of root
CA certificates trusted by Acrobat and Reader for signature validation.
CAs must apply and meet Adobe's requirements to be listed. If a signer's
certificate does not chain to an AATL root, Acrobat shows a warning even
if the signature is cryptographically valid. \\
AcroForm & \textbf{Interactive Form Dictionary.} The PDF structure
(\texttt{/AcroForm} in the document catalog) that contains all form
fields, including signature fields. Defined in ISO 32000-2:2020 Section
12.7. \\
AIA & \textbf{Authority Information Access.} An X.509 v3 certificate
extension (RFC 5280 --- Internet X.509 PKI Certificate and CRL Profile
Section 4.2.2.1) that provides URLs for the OCSP responder and the
issuer's certificate. Used to fetch missing intermediate certificates
during chain building. \\
ASN.1 & \textbf{Abstract Syntax Notation One.} A standard for describing
data structures used in X.509 certificates, CMS, OCSP, and TSP.
Structures are encoded using DER (Distinguished Encoding Rules) for
cryptographic applications. \\
ByteRange & The \texttt{/ByteRange} array in a PDF signature dictionary.
Specifies which bytes of the PDF file are covered by the signature.
Always four integers: \texttt{{[}offset1\ length1\ offset2\ length2{]}}.
See ISO 32000-2:2020 Section 12.8.1. \\
CA & \textbf{Certificate Authority.} An entity that issues X.509
certificates. CAs form a hierarchy: root CAs are self-signed and
explicitly trusted; intermediate CAs are signed by a root or another
intermediate. \\
CAdES & \textbf{CMS Advanced Electronic Signatures.} ETSI standard (EN
319 122) defining enhanced CMS signature profiles for long-term validity
and legal compliance. PAdES builds on CAdES for the CMS portion of PDF
signatures. \\
CMS & \textbf{Cryptographic Message Syntax.} The standard (RFC 5652 ---
Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS)) for signing and encrypting data. PDF
signatures use CMS \texttt{SignedData} as the cryptographic payload
inside the \texttt{/Contents} value. Successor to PKCS\#7. \\
CRL & \textbf{Certificate Revocation List.} A signed list published by a
CA containing the serial numbers of revoked certificates. Defined in RFC
5280 --- Internet X.509 PKI Certificate and CRL Profile Section 5.
Checked during certificate chain validation. \\
DER & \textbf{Distinguished Encoding Rules.} A deterministic binary
encoding of ASN.1 structures. All cryptographic ASN.1 (certificates,
CMS, OCSP, TSP) uses DER encoding. \\
DSS & \textbf{Document Security Store.} A PDF dictionary (\texttt{/DSS}
in the document catalog) defined in ISO 32000-2:2020 Section 12.8.4.3
that stores certificates, CRLs, and OCSP responses needed for offline
(LTV) validation. \\
ECDSA & \textbf{Elliptic Curve Digital Signature Algorithm.} A signature
algorithm based on elliptic curve cryptography. Uses smaller keys than
RSA for equivalent security (e.g., 256-bit ECDSA approximates 3072-bit
RSA). \\
eIDAS & \textbf{Electronic Identification, Authentication, and Trust
Services.} EU Regulation (No 910/2014) that establishes a legal
framework for electronic signatures, seals, and timestamps across EU
member states. Defines three signature levels: simple, advanced, and
qualified. \\
ESS & \textbf{Extended Security Services.} Defined in RFC 2634 and RFC
5035. The \texttt{ESS-signing-certificate-v2} attribute is required in
PAdES signatures to bind the CMS signature to a specific signer
certificate. \\
ETSI & \textbf{European Telecommunications Standards Institute.} The
standards body that publishes PAdES (EN 319 142), CAdES (EN 319 122),
and the validation standards (TS 119 102) referenced by the eIDAS
regulation. \\
EUTL & \textbf{European Union Trusted List.} The official registry of
Qualified Trust Service Providers (QTSPs) maintained by EU member
states. Analogous to Adobe's AATL but for the EU regulatory
framework. \\
HSM & \textbf{Hardware Security Module.} A physical device that stores
private keys and performs cryptographic operations. Used in production
signing environments to protect key material from extraction. \\
Incremental Update & A mechanism defined in ISO 32000-2:2020 Section
7.5.6 that appends new or modified objects to the end of a PDF file
without rewriting the entire file. Every PDF signature is applied via an
incremental update. \\
LTV & \textbf{Long-Term Validation.} The practice of embedding all data
needed for signature validation (certificates, CRLs, OCSP responses)
within the PDF itself, so the signature can be validated offline,
indefinitely, without contacting external servers. \\
MDP & \textbf{Modification Detection and Prevention.} A PDF mechanism
(ISO 32000-2:2020 Section 12.8.2.2) that specifies what changes are
permitted after a certification signature is applied. Three permission
levels exist (P=1, P=2, P=3). \\
OCSP & \textbf{Online Certificate Status Protocol.} A protocol (RFC 6960
--- Online Certificate Status Protocol (OCSP)) for checking certificate
revocation status in real time. An OCSP responder returns \texttt{good},
\texttt{revoked}, or \texttt{unknown} for a given certificate serial
number. \\
OID & \textbf{Object Identifier.} A globally unique dotted-decimal
number (e.g., \texttt{1.2.840.113549.1.7.2}) that identifies algorithms,
attributes, extensions, and other standardized objects in ASN.1
structures. \\
PAdES & \textbf{PDF Advanced Electronic Signatures.} ETSI standard (EN
319 142) that defines constrained profiles for PDF signatures to meet EU
legal requirements. Four baseline profiles: B-B, B-T, B-LT, B-LTA. \\
PKCS\#7 & \textbf{Public-Key Cryptography Standards \#7.} The
predecessor to CMS. PDF signatures historically used the term "PKCS\#7"
(and the SubFilter \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached}), though modern
implementations use CMS. The terms are often used interchangeably. \\
PKCS\#12 & \textbf{Public-Key Cryptography Standards \#12.} A file
format for storing private keys and certificates together, typically
with \texttt{.p12} or \texttt{.pfx} extension. Protected by a
password. \\
PKIX & \textbf{Public-Key Infrastructure (X.509).} The set of standards
and protocols for managing X.509 certificates and building certificate
chains. In Java, \texttt{CertPathBuilder} and \texttt{CertPathValidator}
implement the PKIX algorithm. \\
QTSP & \textbf{Qualified Trust Service Provider.} Under eIDAS, a trust
service provider that has been granted qualified status by an EU member
state's supervisory body. Only QTSPs can issue qualified certificates
and qualified timestamps. \\
RSA & \textbf{Rivest-Shamir-Adleman.} The most widely used public-key
algorithm for digital signatures. Common key sizes for PDF signatures
are 2048 and 4096 bits. \\
SHA-256 & \textbf{Secure Hash Algorithm, 256-bit.} The recommended
minimum hash algorithm for new PDF signatures. Produces a 32-byte
(256-bit) digest. Part of the SHA-2 family. \\
SubFilter & The \texttt{/SubFilter} entry in a PDF signature dictionary
that specifies the CMS encoding format. Values:
\texttt{adbe.pkcs7.detached} (classic), \texttt{ETSI.CAdES.detached}
(PAdES), \texttt{adbe.pkcs7.sha1} (deprecated),
\texttt{adbe.x509.rsa\_sha1} (deprecated). \\
TSA & \textbf{Timestamp Authority.} A trusted server that issues RFC
3161 timestamp tokens. The timestamp token proves that specific data
existed at a specific point in time. \\
TSP & \textbf{Time-Stamp Protocol.} The protocol defined in RFC 3161 ---
Internet X.509 PKI Time-Stamp Protocol (TSP) for requesting and
receiving timestamp tokens from a TSA. \\
TST & \textbf{Time-Stamp Token.} The CMS \texttt{SignedData} structure
returned by a TSA containing a \texttt{TSTInfo} with the timestamp time
and the hash of the timestamped data. \\
VRI & \textbf{Validation Related Information.} A sub-dictionary of the
DSS that groups certificates, CRLs, and OCSP responses for a specific
signature. Keyed by the SHA-1 hash of the signature value. \\
X.509 & The ITU-T standard for public-key certificates. Version 3 (X.509
v3) is used universally in PDF signatures. Defined in RFC 5280 ---
Internet X.509 PKI Certificate and CRL Profile. \\
XAdES & \textbf{XML Advanced Electronic Signatures.} ETSI standard (EN
319 132) for XML-based signatures. Not directly relevant to PDF
signatures, but part of the same eIDAS framework as PAdES and CAdES. \\
\end{longtable}
}

ISO 32000-2:2020 & Document management --- Portable document
format --- Part 2: PDF 2.0 &
\url{https://www.iso.org/standard/75839.html} (paid) or Adobe's
published PDF Reference \\
RFC 5652 & Cryptographic Message Syntax (CMS) &
\url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5652} \\
RFC 5280 & Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Certificate and CRL
Profile & \url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5280} \\
RFC 3161 & Internet X.509 Public Key Infrastructure Time-Stamp Protocol
(TSP) & \url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc3161} \\
RFC 6960 & X.509 Internet Public Key Infrastructure Online Certificate
Status Protocol - OCSP & \url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc6960} \\
RFC 2634 & Enhanced Security Services (ESS) for S/MIME &
\url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc2634} \\
RFC 5035 & Enhanced Security Services (ESS) Update: Adding CertID
Algorithm Agility & \url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5035} \\
RFC 4055 & Additional Algorithms and Identifiers for RSA Cryptography
with SHA-256, SHA-384, and SHA-512 &
\url{https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc4055} \\
ETSI EN 319 142-1 & PAdES: Building blocks and PAdES baseline signatures
& \url{https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_en/319100_319199/31914201/} \\
ETSI EN 319 142-2 & PAdES: Additional PAdES signatures profiles &
\url{https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_en/319100_319199/31914202/} \\
ETSI EN 319 122-1 & CAdES: Building blocks and CAdES baseline signatures
& \url{https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_en/319100_319199/31912201/} \\
ETSI TS 119 102-1 & Electronic Signatures and Infrastructures:
Procedures for Creation and Validation &
\url{https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_ts/119100_119199/11910201/} \\
ETSI TS 119 102-2 & Electronic Signatures and Infrastructures: Signature
Validation Report &
\url{https://www.etsi.org/deliver/etsi_ts/119100_119199/11910202/} \\
EU Regulation 910/2014 & eIDAS: Electronic Identification,
Authentication, and Trust Services &
\url{https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2014/910/oj} \\
EU Regulation 2024/1183 & eIDAS 2.0: Amending Regulation 910/2014
(European Digital Identity Framework) &
\url{https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2024/1183/oj} \\
Adobe AATL & Adobe Approved Trust List --- Technical Requirements &
\url{https://helpx.adobe.com/acrobat/kb/approved-trust-list1.html} \\
\end{longtable}

The RFCs are freely available and are the definitive reference for CMS,
X.509, OCSP, and TSP. The ETSI standards are available free of charge
from ETSI's website after registration. ISO 32000-2 is a paid standard,
but much of the PDF signature specification is reproduced (with
commentary) in this book and in Adobe's freely published PDF Reference
documents.
